Oceans Protection Plan Open - accepting input Transportation Sept 1, 2017 to Mar 31, 2031 2017-09-01 2031-03-31 Transport Canada In November 2016, the $1.5-billion Oceans Protection Plan (OPP) was launched, the largest investment ever made to protect Canada’s coasts and waterways, while also growing our economy. On July 19, 2022, the Prime Minister announced that the Oceans Protection Plan was extended for another 9 years, with the goal of building upon results achieved so far and expanding into new areas. We invite all Canadians to consult the Let’s Talk – Oceans Protection Plan portal and get involved in the discussions on creating stronger Indigenous partnerships and engaging coastal communities, protecting the environment, modernizing our marine safety system and supporting science-based decisions. What We Heard Report available
Participate in the permanent consultation for the revision of the Canadian Research and Development Classification (CRDC) Open - accepting input Health | Science and technology Jan 27, 2025 to Jan 27, 2030 2025-01-27 2030-01-27 Statistics Canada This consultation aims to gather feedback from users who have already implemented the classification, as well as other interested parties who might want to suggest updates or changes to the Canadian Research and Development Classification (CRDC). Federal research funding agencies, Statistics Canada's statistical programs related to R&D data, members of the research community and their partners, and the rest of the Canadian population are invited to provide feedback for the revision of the CRDC. The principal objective of the ongoing consultation is to receive input from classification users to determine if the classification remains relevant and reflective of the Canadian R&D landscape. This ensures that quantitative and qualitative information on research and experimental development, including their socioeconomic objectives, continues to be reliable, timely and relevant for a wide range of audiences. What We Heard Report available
Areas of Interest, Northern Shelf Bioregion (NSB) - Marine Protected Area Network Closed Economic development | Environment | Fisheries | Indigenous Peoples Sept 6, 2022 to Dec 31, 2028 2022-09-06 2028-12-31 Fisheries and Oceans Canada The object of these consultations is to seek public input and advice on the feasibility and regulatory design of three sites moving forward as Areas of Interest for consideration as Oceans Act MPA designation. The sites were identified through a collaboration between the Government of Canada, the Province of BC and 18 First Nations via the Northern Shelf Bioregion (NSB) Network Action Plan. The plan was endorsed by the Minister and leadership from other parties at IMPAC 5 in Vancouver, in February 2023. The three "Areas of Interest" advancing are: Haida Gwaii Offshore, Caamano Sound Douglas Channel and Kitkatla Inlet. - Fishing Sector consultation on the sites are proposed for Fall 2023. - Consultation for Multi-stakeholder advisory committees is proposed to begin in Fall 2023. - Public engagement may begin for each site in fall 2023. The public may be engaged via email, and social media, and other means.
Canada in a Changing Climate Open - accepting input Copyright/Trademarks/Patents | Economy | Environment | Health | Natural resources | Society June 5, 2023 to May 31, 2028 2023-06-05 2028-05-31 Natural Resources Canada Canada in a Changing Climate: Advancing our Knowledge for Action is the national assessment of how and why Canada’s climate is changing; the impacts of these changes on our communities, health, environment, and economy; and how we are adapting. Natural Resources Canada leads Canada in a Changing Climate, a process that depends on the collaboration of a broad partnership of subject-matter experts and assessment users from all orders of government, Indigenous organizations, universities, professional and non-governmental groups, and the private sector. These groups are actively involved in the scoping process for the next cycle of reports, in the report writing teams, and the National Assessment Advisory Committee. The public are also involved, through meetings, conferences, and online engagement tools. Engagement activities commenced May 2023 and will continue throughout the process.
Regulatory Framework Plan Open - accepting input Regulations June 16, 2021 to Dec 31, 2027 2021-06-16 2027-12-31 Canada Energy Regulator The Regulatory Framework Plan (RF Plan) is a tool produced by the CER that supports planning and communication. The RF Plan describes which regulations, guidance material and other regulatory documents we intend to amend or develop over a three year period, and sets out expected timelines for each. The CER welcomes feedback on the content of the Regulatory Framework at any time.
Regulated Industry Engagement Open - accepting input Industry | Regulations Apr 1, 2023 to Dec 31, 2027 2023-04-01 2027-12-31 Canada Energy Regulator The CER is committed to enhancing Canada’s global competitiveness by improving transparency, predictability, and efficiency throughout the regulatory lifecycle, while driving innovation that contributes to the transition to a low carbon economy. The Government of Canada has also signaled its commitment to global competitiveness through the preamble of the Canadian Energy Regulator Act. These commitments will be fulfilled through ongoing dialogue with regulated companies, which will support CER and industry innovation to meet the changing needs of Canadians and the energy sector.
White River First Nations Open - accepting input Indigenous Peoples Feb 1, 2021 to Mar 31, 2027 2021-02-01 2027-03-31 Canada Border Services Agency Engagement with the White River First Nation as part of the Government of Canada delegation to the Recognition of Indigenous Rights and Self Determination Table led by Crown Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Canada. Specifically, a CBSA-led ‘Border Crossing Issues Side Table’ relating to First Nations Border Crossing Issues as examined in the 2017 Minister's Special Representative's report.
Changes to Customs Sufferance Warehouse Regulations with regards to timeframes and means for Warehouse Arrival Certification Message (WACM) Planned Policy | Regulations Apr 1, 2026 to Feb 1, 2027 2026-04-01 2027-02-01 Canada Border Services Agency The Transporter and Cargo Control Programs Unit (TCCPU) is working on resolving two issues involving sufferance warehouse operators sending a Warehouse Arrival Certification Message (WACM). As per Section 14 of the Customs Sufferance Warehouses Regulations (CSWR), when cargo physically arrives at the Customs Sufferance Warehouse, the sufferance warehouse operator must electronically acknowledge the receipt of the shipment (the WACM). Due to the ambiguity of this wording, some operators are not sending the WACMs in a timely manner, or they are sending emails asking the CBSA to electronically acknowledge the receipt of the shipment. We are looking to move forward with amending Section 14 of the CSWR regarding timelines and means of acknowledging the receipt of the goods. We plan to conduct internal and external consultations to gather information.
Indian Oil and Gas Regulation Amendments: Environment and Surface Planned Economic development | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Regulations Oct 1, 2025 to Dec 31, 2026 2025-10-01 2026-12-31 Indigenous Services Canada Amendments to the Indian Oil and Gas Regulations relating to the protection of the Environment and Surface contracts administration
Consultation on Amending the List of Species under the Species at Risk Act: Terrestrial Species January 2026 Open - accepting input Animals | Environment | Plants | Regulations Jan 14, 2026 to Oct 14, 2026 2026-01-14 2026-10-14 Environment and Climate Change Canada Annual consultation on the proposed modifications to the Schedule 1 of the Species at Risk Act (pre-listing consultations).
Modernizing Canada’s Enhanced Feed Ban  Planned Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Regulations June 27, 2026 to Aug 26, 2026 2026-06-27 2026-08-26 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is planning to pre-publish proposed amendments to Canada’s Enhanced Feed Ban (EFB) regulations in the Canada Gazette, Part I (CGI), followed by a 60-day public consultation. These amendments would update the Health of Animals Regulations, the Feeds Regulations, 2024, and the Fertilizers Regulations to allow the repurposing of a subset of specified risk material (SRM) in non-ruminant feed, fertilizer, and pet food. This change will: • Align the EFB with Canada’s internationally recognized Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (BSE) negligible risk status • Ensure consistency with international guidelines • Reduce regulatory red tape and support the economic growth of Canadian businesses
Consultations on the Next Agricultural Policy Framework Open - accepting input Agriculture | Animals | Communication | Development | Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Education | Exporting/Importing | Financial assistance and entitlements | Food and drug | Gender | Health | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | International | Labour | Plants | Policy | Private sector | Rural and remote services | Science and technology | Trade | Training and careers | Youth Jan 20, 2026 to June 30, 2026 2026-01-20 2026-06-30 Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada To consult with Canadians, including stakeholders such as producers, processors, Indigenous communities, women in agriculture, youth, environmental organizations, and small and emerging sectors to develop the successor to the Sustainable Canadian Agricultural Partnership.
Renewal of the 2016 Policy on Results- Engagement with National Indigenous Organizations and Modern Treaty Partners Open - accepting input Indigenous Peoples | Policy Apr 1, 2025 to June 30, 2026 2025-04-01 2026-06-30 Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat TBS is currently undertaking a renewal of the Treasury Board Policy on Results. The renewal has identified several issues regarding results, performance measurement and evaluation in relation to Reconciliation and Indigenous peoples. It is proposed to engage with National Indigenous Organizations and Modern Treaty Partners to incorporate their views into the renewal, and to work towards potentially co-developed solutions to the challenges identified.
Ministerial Advisory Groups • Recognition • Families • Care and Support • Policy • Health and Well-being • Service Excellence and Transition Open - accepting input Policy Nov 1, 2024 to June 30, 2026 2024-11-01 2026-06-30 Veterans Affairs Canada Veterans Affairs Canada has six ministerial advisory groups that provide advice and recommendations to the Minister of Veterans Affairs. For the 2024-2026 session, the advisory groups are: 1. Advisory Group on Recognition working jointly with the Advisory Group on Families 2. Care and Support Advisory Group working jointly with the Policy Advisory Group 3. Health and Well-being Advisory Group 4. Service Excellence and Transition Advisory Group

Amending the Nuclear Liability and Compensation Regulations, pre-publication in CG Part I Planned Justice and the Law | Natural resources | Public safety Mar 4, 2026 to May 3, 2026 2026-03-04 2026-05-03 Natural Resources Canada Pre-publication of amendments to the Nuclear Liability and Compensation Regulations (NLCR) to update existing liability limits for reactors, radioactive waste facilities and nuclear fuel facilities, and to introduce small modular reactors (SMRs) within the framework.

Release of draft (step 2) ICH E22: General Considerations for Patient Preference Studies Open - accepting input Food and drug Jan 12, 2026 to Apr 12, 2026 2026-01-12 2026-04-12 Health Canada Further to the ICH Management Committee’s endorsement of the E22 Concept Paper in June 2024, the E22 Guideline aims to optimise the use of patient preference information as input to pharmaceutical product development using a globally harmonised framework and allow regulatory authorities and the pharmaceutical industry to benefit from harmonised approaches for PPS throughout drug development including, where applicable, submission to different regulatory agencies.
Consultation on 1-methylcyclopropene, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2026-02 Open - accepting input Policy Jan 26, 2026 to Apr 11, 2026 2026-01-26 2026-04-11 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for 1-methylcyclopropene up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 11 April 2026).
REGDOC-3.2.2, Indigenous Engagement Planned Regulations Feb 12, 2026 to Apr 9, 2026 2026-02-12 2026-04-09 Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission This document provides clear expectations for licensees on how to engage with Indigenous communities when a licensee’s proposed project may trigger the Crown’s duty to consult. While this legal duty remains the responsibility of the CNSC, licensees may be asked to carry out parts of the consultation process. The CNSC may use the information and proposed measures submitted by licensees to help meet its consultation obligations.
Consultation on Level 1 and 2 EVSE Inspection Specifications and Procedures Planned Industry | Policy | Regulations Feb 23, 2026 to Apr 9, 2026 2026-02-23 2026-04-09 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada Measurement Canada is consulting on proposed specifications and test procedures for the approval of Level 3 direct current (DC) electric vehicle (EV) charging devices that will be used to charge customers based on the amount of electricity (i.e., by kilowatt-hour or kWh) consumed during an EV charge. Comments provided will be used to finalize the requirements that must be met during verification/reverification inspections.

This consultation supports the Survey on Efficiencies, Legislative Amendments, and the Ongoing Impact of the COVID-19 Pandemic.
Let's talk Pacific Salmon Open - accepting input Fisheries July 15, 2022 to Mar 31, 2026 2022-07-15 2026-03-31 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Regional engagement with the general public, as well as targeted engagement with First Nations, stakeholders, and partners who are interested in Pacific salmon. Feedback received will be documented in the Restoration action Dashboard.

Stakeholders' Forum Open - accepting input Culture Apr 1, 2019 to Mar 31, 2026 2019-04-01 2026-03-31 Library and Archives Canada The members of the Stakeholders' Forum represent the diverse expertise and perspectives of Canada's documentary heritage associations and other organizations.
Services Consultation Committee Open - accepting input Culture Apr 1, 2019 to Mar 31, 2026 2019-04-01 2026-03-31 Library and Archives Canada The mandate of the committee is to give LAC user and professional perspectives, as well as advice, guidance and feedback on the direction and priorities of its public-facing services.
Youth Advisory Council Open - accepting input Culture | Youth Apr 1, 2019 to Mar 31, 2026 2019-04-01 2026-03-31 Library and Archives Canada The Youth Advisory Council helps Library and Archives Canada understand how youth view, access and relate to Canada's documentary heritage.
Engagement: Proposed Indigenous Ministerial Arrangements Regulations under the Canadian Energy Regulator Act Open - accepting input Natural resources Jan 29, 2026 to Mar 31, 2026 2026-01-29 2026-03-31 Natural Resources Canada Natural Resources Canada is engaging Indigenous groups, industry and interested parties to inform the potential development of the proposed IMARs. If regulations are developed, Indigenous governing bodies could enter into Arrangements with the Minister of Energy and Natural Resources to exercise powers, duties, and functions under the Canadian Energy Regulator Act. A discussion paper highlighting key considerations will be published to Let's Talk Natural Resources where public feedback can be submitted. What We Heard Report available
Three-year Departmental Accessibility Plan Open - accepting input Persons with disabilities July 1, 2025 to Mar 31, 2026 2025-07-01 2026-03-31 Public Services and Procurement Canada The Accessible Canada Act and Regulations require federal organizations to post their plans and establish a process that supports transparency and allows individuals to provide feedback by mail, telephone, email or in any manner that the organization uses to communicate with the public. Public insight into the Department’s plans and processes is essential in ensuring that the Department contributes to providing barrier-free communities, workplaces and services for all Canadians. The purpose of this consultation is to solicit feedback from Canadians in the development of recommendations to improve the Department’s accessibility services. The findings will be used to adapt existing activities or initiatives to address accessibility at PSPC and outline commitments for future departmental accessibility plans.
Domestic Assessment of Product Category Filters for the Green Public Procurement Tool (GPPT) Open - accepting input Environment | Government procurement | Industry | Policy Oct 1, 2025 to Mar 31, 2026 2025-10-01 2026-03-31 Public Services and Procurement Canada In collaboration with the Circular Innovation Council (CIC), the project will adapt a platform to align federal procurement practices with Canada’s sustainability objectives, facilitating the transition to a greener economy. The purpose of the consultation is to gather stakeholder feedback on the customization of the Green Public Procurement Tool. The consultation gathers input on product category filters, third-party certifications, and environmental criteria specific to Canadian procurement needs across 20-plus PSPC spending categories. Stakeholder engagement on the customization of the Green Public Procurement Tool (GPPT) is currently being collected via the Green Public Procurement Tool for the Government of Canada – Stakeholder Engagement Survey, which will remain open until March 31, 2026. No further in-person or virtual consultation sessions are scheduled for this phase of the pilot project. Information on consultations and information sessions held to date can be found on the CIC website. Findings from the consultation and survey will inform the customization of the Green Public Procurement Tool to include Canadian-specific products, certifications, and environmental standards across 20+ PSPC spending categories.
Engaging Stakeholders on Economic Abuse Open - accepting input Finance | Indigenous Peoples | Policy | Regulations July 16, 2025 to Mar 31, 2026 2025-07-16 2026-03-31 Women and Gender Equality Canada Seeking insight on how victims and survivors of gender-based violence, including economic abuse, engage with financial institutions, what issues exist, and how these issues could be rectified.

We're looking for feedback, in particular on the approach used to develop the guidelines and on the potential costs of implementing them.

Consultation: Modernizing the framework for clinical trials Open - accepting input Health Dec 20, 2025 to Mar 20, 2026 2025-12-20 2026-03-20 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking feedback on the proposed Clinical Trials Regulations for drugs (pharmaceuticals, biologics and radiopharmaceuticals) and on multiple guidance documents related to the proposal.
General Regulations under the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act Open - accepting input Environment | Natural resources Apr 11, 2023 to Mar 15, 2026 2023-04-11 2026-03-15 Parks Canada Parks Canada is developing general regulations under the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act to help manage national marine conservation areas (NMCAs). Between April and October 2023, Parks Canada engaged with Indigenous organizations, provinces and territories, stakeholders, and the public on the regulatory proposal. A What We Heard report summarizing the input received during the engagement is available on www.letstalknmcas.ca. The draft regulations are expected to be published in Canada Gazette Part I in 2026 for comment. Parks Canada is aiming to publish final regulations in Canada Gazette Part 2 by 2027.
Consultation: Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, Haloacetic Acids Open - accepting input Health Jan 9, 2026 to Mar 13, 2026 2026-01-09 2026-03-13 Health Canada Health Canada has drafted Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, Haloacetic Acids. We're looking for feedback, in particular on the approach used to develop the guidelines and on the potential costs of implementing them.
Canadian experience with vehicle headlights and glare during night-time driving Planned Transportation Jan 12, 2026 to Mar 13, 2026 2026-01-12 2026-03-13 Transport Canada Headlight glare is the most common complaint reported that Transport Canada hears. While trending headlights in newer vehicles may help drivers see better, they may can also cause problems for other drivers. Transport Canada wants to learn how headlight glare affects driving and what vehicle or lighting features may influence how people experience it at night.
Great Slave Lake Integrated Fisheries Management Plan Closed Economy | Environment | Fisheries | Industry | Natural resources Nov 25, 2025 to Mar 1, 2026 2025-11-25 2026-03-01 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Fisheries and Oceans Canada (DFO) will host in-person townhall meetings to gather input from past and present commercial fishers regarding the current state of the commercial fishery on Great Slave Lake (GSL). Feedback from these discussions will inform the development of an Integrated Fisheries Management Plan (IFMP) for Great Slave Lake (GSL) and support a review of existing management measures.
Proposed Regulations Amending the Single-use Plastics Prohibition Regulations Open - accepting input Amendments | Environment | Exporting/Importing Dec 20, 2025 to Feb 28, 2026 2025-12-20 2026-02-28 Environment and Climate Change Canada Environment and Climate Change Canada is seeking comments on proposed amendments to the Single-use Plastics Prohibition Regulations.The proposed amendements would allow the export of the six categories of single-use plastics under the Regulations. This will prevent the loss of Canadian manufacturing jobs and preserve important facility investments made by Canadian companies, while ensuring the Regulations continue to effectively protect the environment from the harms of plastic pollution. Feedback received will be taken into consideration in the development of the final amendments.
Canada Gazette, Part I, Volume 159, Number 51: Order Providing for Reliance on Decisions of, or Documents Produced by, Foreign Regulatory Authorities in Respect of Certain Drugs Open - accepting input Regulations Dec 20, 2025 to Feb 28, 2026 2025-12-20 2026-02-28 Health Canada Notice is given that the Minister of Health proposes to make the annexed Order Providing for Reliance on Decisions of, or Documents Produced by, Foreign Regulatory Authorities in Respect of Certain Drugs under section 30.06 of the Food and Drugs Act. Interested persons may make representations concerning the proposed Order within 70 days after the date of publication of this notice.
Public engagement on how to strengthen federal leadership in emergency management Open - accepting input Gender Nov 28, 2025 to Feb 28, 2026 2025-11-28 2026-02-28 Public Safety Canada The world is changing. Natural disasters in Canada are becoming more frequent and more severe, and the cost to Canadians is going up. This is straining Canada's emergency management system which frequently requires coordinated action from all levels of government. To build a stronger, unified, and resilient system, the Government of Canada is seeking input from Canadians and emergency management partners on priorities, gaps, and opportunities to strengthen federal emergency management in partnership with other jurisdictions. Your input will help inform future decisions and guide actions that will ensure Canada can better prepare, respond, and recover from emergencies.
Horizontal Red Tape Reviews Open - accepting input Economy | Policy | Regulations Dec 3, 2025 to Feb 28, 2026 2025-12-03 2026-02-28 Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat The Red Tape Reduction Office is launching five horizontal red tape reviews to examine key themes and challenges. These reviews will focus on system-wide opportunities to simplify regulation and reduce costs for Canadians and businesses. Each horizontal red tape review will have a theme: 1. Supporting regulatory efficiency for project reviews, 2. Getting products to market faster, 3.Reducing barriers to business productivity, 4. Supporting international trade and greater efficiency at the border, 5. Enhancing regulatory service delivery
Consultation on cold pressed orange oil and Appeal, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2026-01 Open - accepting input Policy Jan 27, 2026 to Feb 25, 2026 2026-01-27 2026-02-25 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), is proposing registration for the sale and use of ORO Orange Oil and Appeal, belonging to ORO Agri Inc., containing the active ingredient cold pressed orange oil, to manage fungal diseases and insect pests on various crops in the greenhouse and field. An evaluation of available scientific information found that, under the approved conditions of use, the health and environmental risks and the value of the pest control products are acceptable.
Proposed updated directive for managing Bacterial Ring Rot (BRR) in seed potatoes Open - accepting input Agriculture | Amendments | Exporting/Importing | Plants | Regulations | Science and technology Jan 26, 2026 to Feb 24, 2026 2026-01-26 2026-02-24 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The purpose of this consultation is to seek feedback on our new directive for managing Bacterial Ring Rot (BRR) in seed potatoes. BRR is a regulated pest under Part II of the Seeds Regulations, and any detection on a seed potato farm will result in the decertification of all seed potatoes on that farm. This makes strong management practices essential to protecting Canada’s seed potato industry.
Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Preservatives to authorize the use of rosemary extract Open - accepting input Food and drug Dec 8, 2025 to Feb 21, 2026 2025-12-08 2026-02-21 Health Canada Rosemary extract is not currently authorized for use as a food additive in foods sold in Canada. As a result of the review of a food additive submission, Health Canada is proposing to: authorize the use of rosemary extract as a preservative as shown in Table 1; set out a requirement for rosemary extract in the Table of Food Additive Specifications as shown in Table 2.
Consultation: Draft guidance on decentralized clinical trials Open - accepting input Health Dec 23, 2025 to Feb 21, 2026 2025-12-23 2026-02-21 Health Canada Health Canada has drafted a guidance document on decentralized clinical trials. The draft guidance outlines regulatory considerations and practical strategies for conducting clinical trials with decentralized elements in Canada. These considerations are set out in Part C, Division 5 of the Food and Drug Regulations and supported by the International Council for Harmonization Guideline for Good Clinical Practice (ICH E6).
Great Bear Gold Project Public notice Participant funding available Open - accepting input Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 21, 2026 to Feb 20, 2026 2026-01-21 2026-02-20 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 21, 2026 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment for the proposed Great Bear Gold Project, a new gold mine located 23 kilometres southeast of Red Lake, Ontario.
Resilient and Adaptive Fisheries Strategy Planned Environment | Fisheries | Industry | Natural resources | Policy Jan 7, 2026 to Feb 18, 2026 2026-01-07 2026-02-18 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Fisheries and Oceans Canada is seeking feedback on the draft Resilient and Adaptive Fisheries Strategy which describes DFO's long-term vision and strategic goals for supporting resilient wild-capture fisheries. The engagement will also solicit ideas for potential actions that could support the resilience of the fishing industry moving forward.
Capital Adequacy Requirements (CAR) Guideline (2027) Open - accepting input Finance Nov 20, 2025 to Feb 18, 2026 2025-11-20 2026-02-18 Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Canada The Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI) has launched a 90-day public consultation on proposed revisions to the Capital Adequacy Requirements (CAR) Guideline (2027). They include several proposed changes to the capital requirements for credit risk and revisions for corporate exposures and for market risk requirements.
Consultation on proposed updates to the Cosmetic Ingredient Hotlist: Prohibited and restricted ingredients Open - accepting input Health | Public safety Nov 19, 2025 to Feb 17, 2026 2025-11-19 2026-02-17 Health Canada Health Canada is proposing to update the Cosmetic Ingredient Hotlist. The proposal includes additions, changes to the conditions for existing entries, and changes to include new substance identifiers or make minor corrections.
Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project Public Notice Participant Funding Available Open - accepting input Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 15, 2026 to Feb 16, 2026 2026-01-15 2026-02-16 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 15, 2026 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment for the proposed Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project, a new open-pit and underground lithium mine located east of Radisson, Quebec.
Proposed Regulations Amending the Migratory Birds Regulations, 2022 - Biennial Hunting Amendments (2026-2028) Open - accepting input Animals | Environment | Regulations Jan 17, 2026 to Feb 15, 2026 2026-01-17 2026-02-15 Environment and Climate Change Canada Environment and Climate Change Canada is seeking comments on proposed amendments to the Migratory Birds Regulations, 2022 to adjust certain hunting areas, open season dates and daily bag and possession limits for migratory bird species by province and territory. This it done every two years to achieve conservation goals and ensure a sustainable harvest of migratory game bird populations.
Fording River Extension Project Public notice Public comments invited on the draft guidelines and plans Open - accepting input Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 15, 2026 to Feb 15, 2026 2026-01-15 2026-02-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 15, 2026 — EVR Operations Limited (the proponent) is proposing to extend the life of its existing Fording River Operations, a metallurgical coal mine located approximately 15 kilometres northeast of Elkford, British Columbia.
Consultation: Proposed new requirements for lithium-ion batteries and consumer products containing lithium-ion batteries under the Canada Consumer Product Safety Act Open - accepting input Health Dec 2, 2025 to Feb 14, 2026 2025-12-02 2026-02-14 Health Canada The purpose of this consultation is to inform interested parties that Health Canada is seeking comments on a proposed regulatory initiative that would introduce mandatory requirements for lithium-ion batteries and consumer products containing lithium-ion batteries under the Canada Consumer Product Safety Act (CCPSA).
Telephone Reporting Sites in areas formerly covered by the Remote Area Border Crossing permit program Open - accepting input Immigration | Indigenous Peoples | Rural and remote services Jan 26, 2026 to Feb 13, 2026 2026-01-26 2026-02-13 Canada Border Services Agency Engagement with local business, Indigenous Communities, municipalities and other stakeholder groups on the location of new telephone reporting sites in remote border areas formerly covered by the Remote Area Border Crossing (RABC) Program
Share your views and ideas: Review of the Environmental Emergency Regulations, 2019 Open - accepting input Environment | Health | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | Policy | Science and technology Dec 15, 2025 to Feb 13, 2026 2025-12-15 2026-02-13 Environment and Climate Change Canada Since the publication of the E2 Regulations in 2019, some industry stakeholders have reported significant challenges around its implementation and effectiveness. Through this consultation, ECCC is engaging with a broader audience to help determine if these, or other challenges, such as regulatory burden, should be considered for regulatory amendments to improve the effectiveness of the E2 Regulations.

(the proponent) is proposing the construction, operation, and closure of a new underground and open-pit gold-silver mine located about 450 kilometres northwest of Prince George and 275 kilometres north of Smithers, British Columbia.

To participate, submit your comments through the Canada Gazette online commenting feature by clicking the link above.
New Nuclear at Wesleyville Project Open - accepting input Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 12, 2026 to Feb 11, 2026 2026-01-12 2026-02-11 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 12, 2026 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada and the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission invite Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description for the New Nuclear at Wesleyville Project and provide feedback by February 11, 2026. Comments received will be used to prepare the summary of issues. Funding is also available to help participate in the impact assessment process for this project. Applications received by February 11, 2026, will be considered
Consultation: Draft guidance on human and veterinary drug submissions based on promising evidence and terms and conditions Open - accepting input Food and drug Dec 12, 2025 to Feb 10, 2026 2025-12-12 2026-02-10 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking feedback on the Draft guidance on human and veterinary drug submissions based on promising evidence and terms and conditions. This document will replace the current Notice of Compliance with conditions (NOC/c) policy and the Policy on terms and conditions for veterinary drugs. It explains the process for drug submissions based on promising evidence of efficacy. It also describes the new authority under the Agile Licensing Regulations, which will allow Health Canada to consider terms and conditions when reviewing these submissions. This authority will come into effect on April 1, 2027. It should be read alongside the Draft guidance on terms and conditions (T&Cs) for human and veterinary drugs.
Consultation: Draft guidance on terms and conditions for human and veterinary drugs Open - accepting input Food and drug Dec 12, 2025 to Feb 10, 2026 2025-12-12 2026-02-10 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking feedback on the Draft guidance on terms and conditions for human and veterinary drugs. This document, previously consulted on in 2022 during the pre-publication of the Agile Licensing draft regulations in Canada Gazette Part I, has been substantially revised. It outlines the scope and application of the new terms and conditions authorities which will come into effect on April 1, 2027.

Consultation: Removal of reconsideration process from requests for priority review and advance consideration for Notice of Compliance with conditions Open - accepting input Health Dec 22, 2025 to Feb 6, 2026 2025-12-22 2026-02-06 Health Canada Health Canada is proposing that negative decisions arising from requests for Priority Review or Advance Consideration for Notice of Compliance with conditions (NOC/c) would no longer be subject to reconsideration requests.
Rural Development Action Plan - Consultation Open - accepting input Economic development | Policy | Rural and remote services Dec 15, 2025 to Feb 6, 2026 2025-12-15 2026-02-06 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada "ISED is developing a Rural Development Action Plan to support rural-inclusive federal initiatives, targeted rural-specific initiatives, and rural stakeholder and partner engagement. To help inform the development of the Rural Development Action Plan, stakeholder views will be sought through this consultation. What we hear from those living in rural Canada and rural stakeholders and partners, be it informed by expertise, lived experience, and/or local or traditional knowledge, will help shape a stronger, more inclusive rural Canada. "

Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Food Enzymes to authorize the use of exo-inulinase Open - accepting input Food and drug | Health Nov 20, 2025 to Feb 3, 2026 2025-11-20 2026-02-03 Health Canada The purpose of this notice is to publicly announce Health Canada’s intention to permit the use of exo-inulinase from Trichoderma reesei strain AR-577 as a food enzyme in the foods and under the conditions specified in this notice. Comments on this proposal can be submitted by email to food.ibr-ipr.aliments@hc-sc.gc.ca until February 3, 2026. Please use the words "exo-inulinase (P-FAA-25-01)" in the subject line of your email.
Consultation on spidoxamat, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-27 Open - accepting input Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Nov 18, 2025 to Feb 1, 2026 2025-11-18 2026-02-01 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for spidoxamat up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 1 February 2026).
Commercial Air Services Standards Omnibus Package Planned Transportation Dec 1, 2025 to Feb 1, 2026 2025-12-01 2026-02-01 Transport Canada The 2023 ICAO USOAP audit of the CARs and associated standards outlined several areas of non-alignment with ICAO norms. Transport Canada, Civil Aviation (TCCA) has been working on developing standard amendments to address audit findings in a manner that is effective in improving international alignment. The Commercial Air Services Standards were found to contain several areas of non-alignment which are partly being addressed through the present NPA package.
Canada-NS and Canada-NL Offshore Renewable Energy Regulations Open - accepting input Environment | Natural resources May 1, 2022 to Jan 31, 2026 2022-05-01 2026-01-31 Natural Resources Canada These regulations will support the implementation of Part 3 of the Accord Acts, as amended by Bill C-49, and will establish requirements to ensure the safety and environmental protection of offshore renewable energy projects in the two offshore areas that are jointly managed with Nova Scotia and Newfoundland and Labrador.
Contribute to Statistics Canada’s consultative engagement on the Residential Building Occupancy Project Open - accepting input Housing Oct 23, 2025 to Jan 31, 2026 2025-10-23 2026-01-31 Statistics Canada n an important step towards solving Canada’s housing crisis, Statistics Canada is evaluating ways to collect more comprehensive and precise housing data. The Residential Building Occupancy (RBO) Project aims to address gaps and limitations in how housing construction-related information is collected and reported across Canada. It focuses on enhancing timeliness, accuracy, and integration of data sources to better inform decision-making. The scope of the RBO project is to improve the process of how this data is captured, standardized, and compared across jurisdictions. The project will develop more streamlined approaches and explore automated solutions. The aim is to increase efficiency in data collection and improve usability for both data providers and data users. As part of this initiative, a GCform was created to gather feedback from municipalities on the type, format, and availability of residential building occupancy data they currently collect.
Consultation on the Application for Authorization of the Lac-Mégantic Rail Bypass Project Open - accepting input Transportation Nov 12, 2025 to Jan 30, 2026 2025-11-12 2026-01-30 Canadian Transportation Agency The Canadian Transportation Agency (Agency) invites the public to submit comments on the application filed on September 19, 2025, by the Canadian Pacific Railway Company (CPKC). This application is to construct a 12.5 km long railway line with the aim of moving railway operations outside downtown Lac-Mégantic (Lac-Mégantic rail bypass).
Driving Effective Carbon Markets in Canada Open - accepting input Economic development | Environment Dec 18, 2025 to Jan 30, 2026 2025-12-18 2026-01-30 Environment and Climate Change Canada The paper is intended to seek constructive feedback on strengthening and improving the benchmark while maintaining flexibility for provincial and territorial system design.
Valentine Gold Project Public notice Public Comments Invited on Proposed Amendments to the Decision Statement Open - accepting input Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 9, 2026 to Jan 30, 2026 2026-01-09 2026-01-30 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 9, 2026 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period on proposed amendments to the decision statement for the Valentine Gold Project, an open-pit gold mine located at Valentine Lake, approximately 55 kilometres southwest of Millertown in Newfoundland and Labrador.
Engagement: Designated Officer Regulations under the Canadian Energy Regulator Act Open - accepting input Natural resources Dec 17, 2025 to Jan 30, 2026 2025-12-17 2026-01-30 Natural Resources Canada Natural Resources Canada is seeking comments regarding proposed regulations under the Canadian Energy Regulator Act (CERA). The Designated Officer Regulations would specify technical and administrative functions to be carried out by designated officers within the Canadian Energy Regulator – employees of the Regulator with expertise in their field. A discussion paper will be published on Let’s Talk NRCan’s website outlining the approach to these regulations.
Share your thoughts: Proposal to deregulate Phytophthora abietivora in Canada Open - accepting input Agriculture | Environment | Exporting/Importing | Plants | Policy | Regulations Nov 18, 2025 to Jan 29, 2026 2025-11-18 2026-01-29 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The CFIA is seeking feedback on the deregulation of P. abietivora and to add P. abietivora to the list of Phytophthora species that are not regulated by Canada.
Consultation on iodosulfuron-methyl-sodium and its associated end-use product, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-10 Open - accepting input Food and drug | Health Oct 30, 2025 to Jan 28, 2026 2025-10-30 2026-01-28 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of iodosulfuron-methyl-sodium and associated end-use products registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed updates to standard label statements. Iodosulfuron-methyl-sodium is a selective sulfonylurea herbicide used to control a range of post-emergence grassy and broadleaf weeds in field corn. It is applied once a year by field sprayer.

Consulting Canadians on a potential Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement with India Open - accepting input Economic development | Exporting/Importing | Foreign affairs | International | Policy | Trade Dec 13, 2025 to Jan 27, 2026 2025-12-13 2026-01-27 Global Affairs Canada In anticipation of a commencement of trade agreement negotiations with India in 2026, Global Affairs Canada is conducting public consultations to invite Canadians to submit their views, reflections and priorities with respect to an enhanced trade and investment with India. Submissions received from these consultations will help inform Canada’s interests in, and approach to, CEPA negotiations with India.

Flipi Gas-Fired Generation Project Public notice Comments invited on the summary of the Initial Project Description and funding available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 7, 2026 to Jan 27, 2026 2026-01-07 2026-01-27 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 7, 2026 – Ottawa, Ontario TransAlta Corporation is proposing to develop a new power-generating facility fueled by natural gas, located approximately 18 kilometres southwest of Rimbey, Alberta. As proposed, the Flipi Gas-Fired Generation Project would have a production capacity of 460 megawatts and use combined cycle technology. The project is expected to operate for 30 years. The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) invites Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description and provide comments on the proposed project.

Consultation: Proposed risk-based approach for the authorization of infant food for a special dietary purpose Closed Food and drug Nov 26, 2025 to Jan 25, 2026 2025-11-26 2026-01-25 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking comments on its proposed risk-based approach for the authorization of infant food for a special dietary purpose. These foods are regulated under Division 25 of the Food and Drug Regulations and include infant formula and medical foods for infants (e.g., human milk fortifiers).
Potential regulatory amendments to the Valuation for Duty Regulations Closed Finance | Industry | International | Justice and the Law | Private sector | Regulations | Trade Dec 3, 2025 to Jan 23, 2026 2025-12-03 2026-01-23 Canada Border Services Agency Amendments to the Valuation for Duty Regulations to define the term “sold for export to Canada” and address the existing regulatory loophole that unduly benefits non-resident importers (NRIs) with minimal operations and investments in Canada.
Consultation on proposed harmonized requirements for the evaluation of software in software-controlled measuring devices Open - accepting input Industry | International | Policy | Regulations | Science and technology | Trade Oct 14, 2025 to Jan 23, 2026 2025-10-14 2026-01-23 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada Measurement Canada will conduct public consultation on the new harmonized requirements for the evaluation of software on measurement devices approved for use in trade. This will be an opportunity for stakeholders to learn about these new and updated requirements in the approval process for the devices in Canada and provide their comments.
Consultation on proposed enhancements to the Scientific Research Experimental Development (SR&ED) program Closed Taxes Jan 9, 2026 to Jan 22, 2026 2026-01-09 2026-01-22 Canada Revenue Agency The CRA hosted virtual meetings with some of the organizations that participated in Phase 1 of the consultation to present the draft pathway application process and gather their feedback.
Consultation: Comment period for the danger to human health or safety assessment for products containing button or coin batteries and the packaging of button and coin batteries Closed Health | Public safety Nov 24, 2025 to Jan 22, 2026 2025-11-24 2026-01-22 Health Canada Health Canada invites stakeholders to provide comments on this danger to human health or safety assessment. Health Canada will consider these comments when making a determination on whether there is a danger to human health or safety posed by products that do not meet this safety criteria.
Consultation on Nealta Miticide, containing cyflumetofen, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-17 Closed Agriculture | Health | Plants Dec 8, 2025 to Jan 22, 2026 2025-12-08 2026-01-22 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Nealta Miticide, containing the technical grade active ingredient cyflumetofen, to control spider mites on greenhouse ornamentals. This evaluation was completed under the User Requested Minor Use Label Expansion program, which is a cooperative program between Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada and Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency and includes participation by sponsors groups, manufacturers, and both provincial and federal governments.
Mihta Askiy Data Center Project Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 2, 2026 to Jan 22, 2026 2026-01-02 2026-01-22 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 2, 2026 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is inviting Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description for the Mihta Askiy Data Center Project and provide feedback by January 22, 2026. Comments received will be used to prepare the summary of issues. Funding is also available to help participate in the impact assessment process for this project. Applications received by February 2, 2026, will be considered.
Consultation on DEET plus related active toluamides and its associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-09 Closed Food and drug | Health Oct 23, 2025 to Jan 21, 2026 2025-10-23 2026-01-21 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting the public for feedback on the proposed re-evaluation decision for DEET plus related active toluamides and associated end-use products (DEET). Re-evaluations are cyclical reviews that determine if the pesticide meets current health, environment and value requirements. Re-evaluations are conducted using modern assessment techniques and current scientific information. There have been no changes to DEET products since the previous re-evaluation in 2002. Based on the latest scientific information, Health Canada scientists have completed all necessary evaluations to assess the health and environmental risks and value of DEET. As a result of these evaluations, Health Canada is proposing labelling updates to bring these up to current labelling standards and ensure the continued protection of people and the environment.
Consultation on ethiprole, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-23 Closed Food and drug | Health Nov 7, 2025 to Jan 21, 2026 2025-11-07 2026-01-21 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for ethiprole up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 21 January 2026).
Consultation on quizalofop-ethyl, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-24 Closed Food and drug | Health Nov 7, 2025 to Jan 21, 2026 2025-11-07 2026-01-21 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for quizalofop-ethyl up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 21 January 2026).
Proposed amended livestock feed ingredient – DL-Methionine hydroxy analogue isopropyl ester Open - accepting input Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Dec 19, 2025 to Jan 19, 2026 2025-12-19 2026-01-19 Canadian Food Inspection Agency We intend to amend the existing SIF description for DL-methionine hydroxy analogue isopropyl ester under subclass 5.1.2 (Amino acids excluding those from fermentation processes) of class 5 (Nutritional ingredients) in part 2 of the CFIT. The amended description expands the approved use (a source of methionine activity) in diets for lactating dairy sheep, and lactating dairy goats.
Canada Gazette, Part I, Volume 159, Number 45: Regulations Amending the Medical Devices Regulations (Establishment Licences) Closed Food and drug | Health Nov 8, 2025 to Jan 17, 2026 2025-11-08 2026-01-17 Health Canada Health Canada is proposing amendments to further modernize the MDR, while maintaining oversight of the supply of medical devices in Canada. These proposed amendments would facilitate better international alignment, close certain compliance and enforcement gaps observed amongst industry and work to reduce the administrative burden, while maintaining traceability on the supply of devices through the Canadian market.
Point Rousse Port Expansion Project Public Notice Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Dec 12, 2025 to Jan 16, 2026 2025-12-12 2026-01-16 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada December 12, 2025 - Ottawa, ON - The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) invites Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description and provide comments on the proposed project by January 16, 2026. This feedback will help IAAC prepare a summary of issues that will be given to the proponent.
Independent Review of the Preclearance Act, 2016 Planned Public safety Nov 26, 2025 to Jan 16, 2026 2025-11-26 2026-01-16 Public Safety Canada Per section 62.1 of the Preclearance Act, 2016 the Minister of Public Safety must cause to be done an independent review of this Act, its administration and operations, 5 years after the Act comes into force. On August 14, 2024, the Minister of Public Safety directed Public Safety Canada to make arrangements for the independent review. InterVISTAS Consulting Inc. has been selected to undertake the independent review. Engagement methodogies are expected to be mixed at the discretion of the independent reviewer.
Targeted amendments to the Clean Fuel Regulations Closed Amendments | Economy | Environment | Industry | Regulations Dec 3, 2025 to Jan 15, 2026 2025-12-03 2026-01-15 Environment and Climate Change Canada Environment and Climate Change Canada is asking industry, stakeholders and Canadians to provide feedback on targeted amendments to the Clean Fuel Regulations (CFR) to strengthen the resiliency and support the development of Canada’s low-carbon fuel sector, while maintaining the Regulations’ primary focus on lowering greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, and transitioning to a low carbon economy.
Consultation on benzoic acid and Microban Additive GS, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-18 Closed Health Dec 16, 2025 to Jan 15, 2026 2025-12-16 2026-01-15 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Ascera X-TA and Microban Additive GS belonging to Microban Canada Inc., containing the technical grade active ingredient benzoic acid, for use as a material preservative to inhibit the growth of bacteria, mould, mildew, fungus and yeast in products with household, institutional, and industrial uses including non-food contact polymers and plastics, textiles, paints, industrial maintenance coatings, and hygroscopic inorganic, cellulose materials.
Point Rousse Port Expansion Project Public notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Dec 16, 2025 to Jan 15, 2026 2025-12-16 2026-01-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada December 16, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for the proposed Point Rousse Port Expansion Project, which would expand the existing port infrastructure at Point Rousse, located on the Baie Verte Peninsula, in Newfoundland and Labrador.
Release of draft (step 2) ICH Guideline Q3E: Impurities: Guideline for Extractables and Leachables Closed Food and drug | Health Oct 17, 2025 to Jan 14, 2026 2025-10-17 2026-01-14 Health Canada The Q3E Guideline outlines the assessment and control of extractables and leachables (E&L) and is expected to assist both applicants and regulators by providing focus on critical aspects, and improving transparency in requirements for medicinal products including drug delivery device components.
Bécancour Port Terminal Expansion Project – B6 Wharf Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Nov 26, 2025 to Jan 12, 2026 2025-11-26 2026-01-12 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada November 26, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is making funding available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for this project. Applications received by January 12, 2026, will be considered.
Amendments to Superintendent's directives relating to cybersecurity guidelines, minimum requirements and measures for the Licensed Insolvency Trustee community Planned Amendments | Policy Dec 12, 2025 to Jan 12, 2026 2025-12-12 2026-01-12 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada The Office of the Superintendent of Bankruptcy (OSB) is looking to consult with stakeholders on amendments to be made to Superintendent's directives, as needed, relating to cybersecurity guidelines, minimum requirements and measures for the Licensed Insolvency Trustee community.
Share your thoughts: Proposed new and amended livestock feed ingredients – New T. reesei and amended T. longibrachiatum ingredients Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Dec 10, 2025 to Jan 9, 2026 2025-12-10 2026-01-09 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is seeking comments on the following proposed new single ingredient feed (SIF) description for T. reesei. In addition, the CFIA is seeking comments on the amended description for T. longibrachiatum.
Public engagement on management measures in the Newfoundland and Labrador (NL) Recreational Groundfish Fishery Closed Fisheries | Regulations | Recreation Nov 17, 2025 to Jan 9, 2026 2025-11-17 2026-01-09 Fisheries and Oceans Canada This survey seeks public input on the management of the Newfoundland and Labrador Recreational Groundfish Fishery, including views on current practices, potential regulatory changes, and future management options. Results will be considered as part of the 2026 management decision.
Matane Port Facilities Expansion Project Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Nov 25, 2025 to Jan 9, 2026 2025-11-25 2026-01-09 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada November 25, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is making funding available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for this project. Applications received by January 9, 2026, will be considered.
Consultation on 2-phenethyl propionate, nuranone, geraniol, eugenol, and Japanese Beetle Trap Refill, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-14 Closed Food and drug | Health Dec 5, 2025 to Jan 4, 2026 2025-12-05 2026-01-04 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of 2-Phenethyl Propionate Technical, Nuranone Technical, Geraniol Technical, Eugenol Technical, and related end-use product, Japanese Beetle Trap Refill, containing the active ingredients 2-phenethyl propionate, nuranone, geraniol, and eugenol, to attract and trap Japanese beetles in outdoor domestic areas.
Consultation on formic acid and FENNOSURF 600-C, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-16 Closed Food and drug | Health Dec 5, 2025 to Jan 4, 2026 2025-12-05 2026-01-04 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of FENNOSURF 600-T and FENNOSURF 600-C, belonging to Kemira Water Solutions, Inc., containing the active ingredient formic acid, for bacterial control in municipal wastewater treatment and the manufacturing process of paper and paperboard.
Consultation on Automatic tax filing Closed Taxes Sept 9, 2025 to Dec 31, 2025 2025-09-09 2025-12-31 Canada Revenue Agency The CRA is consulting to inform the future of automatic tax filing in Canada to continue its ongoing efforts to ensure more Canadians with lower-income receive the benefits and credits designed to support them.
Banc-des-Américains Oceans Act Marine Protected Area Management Plan Closed Environment | Fisheries | Regulations Jan 11, 2023 to Dec 31, 2025 2023-01-11 2025-12-31 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Fisheries and Oceans Canada is seeking comments from First Nations, other governments, communities, industry and other stakeholders on this Management Plan.
Indigenous Ministerial Arrangements Regulations Open - accepting input Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Regulations July 25, 2022 to Dec 31, 2025 2022-07-25 2025-12-31 Natural Resources Canada The Indigenous Ministerial Arrangements Regulations are a feature of the Canadian Energy Regulator Act that would enable the Minister to enter into arrangements with Indigenous governing bodies for carrying out the purposes of the Act
Visitor Use Management - Lake Louise Area Open - accepting input Recreation | Seniors | Society | Science and technology | Transportation | Youth June 17, 2024 to Dec 31, 2025 2024-06-17 2025-12-31 Parks Canada Banff National Park is developing a visitor use management plan to adapt to increasing visitation in the Lake Louise area. Visitation to the Lake Louise rose 31% over the past 10 years and is expected to continue to rise. More people than ever before are visiting and connecting with this special place. Higher numbers of people on the landscape are causing challenges like congestion, crowding, human-wildlife conflict, and impacts to ecosystems. It also makes emergency response more difficult. Parks Canada has used many tools to address these challenges including shuttles, reservations services, paid parking, and marketing campaigns. We’ve analyzed available data and learned over the years—now, we’d like to hear from you.
Updating the Community Plans for Field, B.C. and Lake Louise, Alberta Open - accepting input Communication | Environment May 29, 2024 to Dec 31, 2025 2024-05-29 2025-12-31 Parks Canada Parks Canada is updating the Field Community Plan and the Lake Louise Community Plan. National park community plans are one of the documents used by Parks Canada, stakeholders, leaseholders and other interested parties to guide the development and management of communities administered by Parks Canada. A community plan provides a long-term vision for a sustainable future that integrates a healthy natural environment with economic and social well-being. The updated plans will help guide these communities into the future (over the next 10+ years) with renewed community vision statements and other key elements that reflect current trends and changes that have occurred over the last ten years in the communities, park, region and across the country.
Share your feedback on Census of Environment products Open - accepting input Environment Sept 25, 2025 to Dec 31, 2025 2025-09-25 2025-12-31 Statistics Canada The Census of Environment (CoE) program is committed to providing comprehensive biophysical information on the extent and condition of Canada’s ecosystems, linking it to socio-economic data and informing Canadians about the many values of nature's ecosystem services This integrated approach is designed to support policy development and investment decisions at national, provincial, and regional levels. As a relatively new program, the CoE would like to gather feedback on client satisfaction with its products and tools. This input will help assess the usefulness of the program’s outputs during its first five years and inform future planning and improvements. As part of this initiative, an e-form was created to gather users’ perspectives, relevance, usability, accessibility, quality and impact of CoE products.
Consultation: Adding melatonin for sleep-related use in the pediatric population to the Prescription Drug List Closed Food and drug | Health Sept 16, 2025 to Dec 30, 2025 2025-09-16 2025-12-30 Health Canada The purpose of this consultation is to communicate to industry, practitioners and the public about Health Canada's proposed approach to regulating melatonin products that would be intended for sleep-related use in the pediatric population as prescription drugs under the Food & Drug Regulations. We want to provide an opportunity for the public and other interested stakeholders to comment on the proposal to revise the listing for "Melatonin or its salts" on the human use part of the Prescription Drug List (PDL). Specifically, the proposal is to broaden the qualifier to capture all sleep-related uses in the pediatric population (i.e., all children and adolescents under 18 years of age). The intent of this proposal is to help ensure healthcare practitioner oversight on the use of melatonin products for sleep-related purposes in children and adolescents under 18 years of age.
Proposed amended livestock feed ingredient – Polymethylolcarbamide Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Nov 28, 2025 to Dec 29, 2025 2025-11-28 2025-12-29 Canadian Food Inspection Agency We intend to amend the existing SIF description for polymethylolcarbamideunder subclass 6.4 (Pelleting aids) of class 6 (Non-nutritive ingredients) in part 2 of the CFIT. The amended description will contain all approved livestock species for which this SIF has been approved for use as a pelleting aid in finished pellets.
Proposed amended livestock feed ingredient – L-Ascorbyl-2-polyphosphate Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Nov 28, 2025 to Dec 29, 2025 2025-11-28 2025-12-29 Canadian Food Inspection Agency We intend to amend the existing SIF description for L-ascorbyl-2-polyphosphate under subclass 5.4.2 (Vitamins excluding those from fermentation processes) of class 5 (Nutritional ingredients) in part 1 of the CFIT. The amended description will contain all approved species for which this SIF has been approved for.
Proposed new livestock feed ingredient – Micro-algae fermentation oil Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Nov 28, 2025 to Dec 29, 2025 2025-11-28 2025-12-29 Canadian Food Inspection Agency We intend to add micro-algae fermentation oil under subclass 6.16.2 (Food modifying ingredients – Microbial) of class 6 (Non-nutritive ingredients) in part 2 of the CFIT 
Evaluation by Environment and Climate Change Canada of the ongoing need for the Emergency Order for the Protection of the Western Chorus Frog (Longueuil) Open - accepting input Environment Sept 27, 2025 to Dec 29, 2025 2025-09-27 2025-12-29 Environment and Climate Change Canada Environment and Climate Change Canada is seeking information through email and mail to inform its evaluation of whether there is an ongoing need for the emergency order in place to protect Western Chrorus Frog in Longueuil, Quebec.
Consultation on iron (present as Ferric Sodium EDTA Trihydrate) and associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-08 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Sept 26, 2025 to Dec 25, 2025 2025-09-26 2025-12-25 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of iron (present as Ferric Sodium EDTA Trihydrate) and associated end-use products registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed updates to standard label statements.
Draft Assessment for Manganese and its Compounds, and the Risk Management Scope Open - accepting input Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Oct 25, 2025 to Dec 24, 2025 2025-10-25 2025-12-24 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Draft assessment of manganese and its compounds and the Risk Management Scope published in the Canada Gazette, Part 1, Volume 159, Number 43 on October 25, 2025
Consultation on isocycloseram, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-21 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Oct 9, 2025 to Dec 23, 2025 2025-10-09 2025-12-23 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for isocycloseram up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 23 December 2025).
Consultation: Draft guidance on preparing and submitting summary reports for marketed drugs and natural health products Closed Food and drug | Health Oct 24, 2025 to Dec 23, 2025 2025-10-24 2025-12-23 Health Canada Health Canada is holding a 60-day consultation on: revisions to the Preparing and Submitting Summary Reports for Marketed Drugs and Natural Health Products - Guidance Document for Industry, now entitled “Guidance on preparing and submitting summary reports for marketed drugs and natural health products”; Draft form: Notifying Health Canada of a significant change in what is known about the risks and benefits of a product.
Draft Contractor Oversight Guidance Open - accepting input Amendments | Industry | Natural resources | Regulations Sept 22, 2025 to Dec 21, 2025 2025-09-22 2025-12-21 Canada Energy Regulator This guidance clarifies existing Onshore Pipeline Regulation (OPR) requirements related to contractor oversight and is intended to help companies achieve compliance and prevent harm. This guidance does not introduce any new requirements. This draft guidance will be amended following this consultation process and will be updated as needed to align with future regulatory changes. Under our OPR, companies must have management system processes to identify, evaluate, prevent, manage, and mitigate hazards and risks, reducing the potential for harm. Effective contractor oversight is one of many essential controls that help achieve this goal. The final Contractor Oversight Guidance is anticipated to be released in early 2026. The CER is considering a future workshop with CER-regulated pipeline companies to discuss the final guidance document and explore industry best practices and challenges related to contractor oversight.
Amendments to Directive No 2R2, Joint Filing, and to various forms Planned Amendments | Policy Nov 21, 2025 to Dec 21, 2025 2025-11-21 2025-12-21 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada The Office of the Superintendent of Bankruptcy (OSB) is looking to consult with stakeholders on amendments to be made to Directive No 2R2, Joint Filing, and to various forms.
Consultations for the 2026 Annual Report on the 2030 Agenda and the Sustainable Development Goals Closed Children | Culture | Development | Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Education | Food and drug | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Housing | Health | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | International | Natural resources | Persons with disabilities | Policy | Regulations | Seniors | Service | Society | Training and careers | Youth Nov 4, 2025 to Dec 19, 2025 2025-11-04 2025-12-19 Employment and Social Development Canada Feedback from external stakeholders and partners is to be gathered to inform the 2026 Annual Report on the 2030 Agenda and the Sustainable Development Goals.
Building Canada Strong: Youth in the Labour Market Closed Employment | Youth Nov 24, 2025 to Dec 19, 2025 2025-11-24 2025-12-19 Employment and Social Development Canada This online engagement process invites stakeholders and partners with youth employment knowledge to offer perspectives on challenges faced by youth in the Canadian labour market and key priorities for action to tackle youth unemployment.
Contribute to Statistics Canada's Web modernization Journey Open - accepting input Communication | Economy | Employment | Environment | Education | Exporting/Importing | Finance | Housing | Health | Immigration | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | Labour | Natural resources | Persons with disabilities | Public safety | Seniors | Society | Science and technology | Trade | Transportation | Youth May 20, 2025 to Dec 19, 2025 2025-05-20 2025-12-19 Statistics Canada As part of its modernization journey, Statistics Canada is dedicated to the continual improvement of our services and products. The purpose of this engagement activity is to obtain input from Canadian organizations that rely heavily on the Statistics Canada website, focusing on topics such as user experience, design, tools and content. This feedback will further inform and guide our digital modernization efforts. The primary objectives of this engagement on the Statistics Canada website are to: Identify how users find information on the website and what tools and strategies they rely on. Assess new design options. Identify preferences for content types and formats. Gather feedback on website readability and language-use. Capture user comments on various aspects of the website.
Consultation on spidoxamat, Velancor, Plenexos Smart, Plenexos Care, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-15 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Nov 18, 2025 to Dec 18, 2025 2025-11-18 2025-12-18 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Spidoxamat Technical Insecticide, Velancor, and Plenexos Care, containing the technical grade active ingredient spidoxamat, and Plenexos Smart, containing the technical grade active ingredients spidoxamat and flupyradifurone, to control labelled pests on pome fruits, stone fruits, small fruits vine climbing (except fuzzy kiwifruit) and tree nuts (except almonds).
Share your input on the dissemination approach for data on transgender children and youth from the 2021 Census of Population Closed Children | Gender Based Analysis | Youth Nov 10, 2025 to Dec 18, 2025 2025-11-10 2025-12-18 Statistics Canada Statistics Canada held targeted engagement with subject-matter experts and individuals with lived experience to inform a dissemination approach for 2021 Census data on transgender and non-binary children and youth aged 14 years and under in Canada.
Notice with respect to the availability of the proposed environmental occurrences notification agreements between Canada and Alberta, Canada and British Columbia, Canada and Manitoba, Canada and Ontario, Canada and Saskatchewan, and Canada and Yukon Closed Environment | Industry | Policy | Public safety | Regulations Oct 18, 2025 to Dec 17, 2025 2025-10-18 2025-12-17 Environment and Climate Change Canada The environmental occurrences notification agreements have a maximum term of 5 years, after which they must be renewed. The current Notification Agreements expire March 24, 2026. The proposed renewed Notification Agreements are available in the Canada Gazette, Part 1 for a 60-day comment period.

Have your say on the draft co-operation agreement between Ontario and Canada - Public comments invited Public Notice Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Nov 24, 2025 to Dec 15, 2025 2025-11-24 2025-12-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada November 24, 2025 – Ottawa, ON – Impact Assessment Agency of Canada What is happening? The Government of Canada committed in May 2025 to striking co-operation agreements with every interested province within six months. These agreements will help achieve the Government’s goal of "one project, one review" while upholding Canada’s world-leading environmental standards and constitutional obligations to Indigenous Peoples.
Have your say on the draft co-operation agreement between Manitoba and Canada - Public comments invited Public Notice Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Nov 24, 2025 to Dec 15, 2025 2025-11-24 2025-12-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada November 24, 2025 – Ottawa, ON – Impact Assessment Agency of Canada What is happening?
