Consultation: Renewal of the Canadian Task Force on Preventive Health Care Closed Health Nov 14, 2025 to Dec 14, 2025 2025-11-14 2025-12-14 Public Health Agency of Canada We want to hear your thoughts on the renewal of the Canadian Task Force on Preventive Health Care (Task Force). Your feedback will help to ensure effective implementation of the recommendations from the External Expert Review and inform a renewed Task Force that is credible, forward-looking and grounded in the highest standards of scientific excellence, equity and public trust.
Bécancour Port Terminal Expansion Project – B6 Wharf Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Nov 24, 2025 to Dec 13, 2025 2025-11-24 2025-12-13 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada November 24, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is inviting Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description and provide feedback related to the proposed project by December 13, 2025. Comments received will be used to prepare the summary of issues.
Share your experiences with the Labour Force Survey Open - accepting input Labour Oct 1, 2025 to Dec 12, 2025 2025-10-01 2025-12-12 Statistics Canada The Labour Force Survey (LFS) is the source of Canada’s official unemployment rate and one of the primary sources of labour market information for Canadians and policy makers. Statistics Canada’s Centre for Labour Market Information is undertaking a series of studies to better understand respondent experience with the LFS. These studies are conducted in collaboration with the Privy Council Office’s Impact and Innovation Unit and will include one-on-one and group discussions with individuals who have recently completed their participation in the LFS.The engagement activity will gather feedback from those who had been selected to participate in the LFS to better understand their motivations for participation, barriers to timely participation and reactions to LFS communications. Topics of discussion will include trust in Statistics Canada, the clarity and tone of communication materials, and the usability of different survey completion modes. The findings will inform upcoming initiatives to enhance respondent experiences and lay the groundwork for future experiments and interventions aimed at improving survey operations.
PICA 2025-001: Use of Foreign Ex-Military Aircraft in Canadian Commercial Aviation Open - accepting input Transportation Oct 2, 2025 to Dec 12, 2025 2025-10-02 2025-12-12 Transport Canada This PICA seeks more information to support decision-making as to whether, or the degree to which, Canada should allow operation of ex-military aircraft in domestic commercial aviation.
Clyde River Article 16 Options Summary Meeting Closed Culture | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Education | Fisheries | Indigenous Peoples | Science and technology Dec 10, 2025 to Dec 11, 2025 2025-12-10 2025-12-11 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Fisheries and Oceans (DFO) Fisheries Management Marine Conservation Targets will be holding a meeting with communities to discuss fisheries potential in each of the affected Tallurutiup Imanga (TI) National Marine Conservation Area communities and the status of Article 16 of the Inuit Impact and Benefit Agreeement for TI. Will be presenting infomation to date as well as to fill any gaps in community priority related to fisheries development.
Newfoundland and Labrador Aquatic Invasive Species (AIS) Advisory Committee Planned Animals | Environment | Fisheries | Industry Dec 10, 2025 to Dec 10, 2025 2025-12-10 2025-12-10 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Annual meeting of stakeholders with interests in the presence, impact, management, and research related to aquatic invasive species. The meeting is organized and co-chaired by Fisheries and Oceans (DFO) (AIS - Science) and the Province of Newfoundland and Labrador, with the goal of presenting current related work, planned research, and priority setting for the region on AIS issues.

Proposed Modification to the Conditions of Licence on Space Debris Mitigation Planned Amendments | Communication | Industry | Policy | Private sector | Regulations | Science and technology Dec 10, 2024 to Dec 10, 2025 2024-12-10 2025-12-10 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada Through the release of this paper, Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED), on behalf of the Minister of Innovation, Science and Industry (the Minister), is hereby initiating a consultation on updating the current non-geostationary satellite orbit (NGSO) licensing requirements and conditions of licence (CoL) related to the mitigation of orbital debris.
Project 6 - All-Season Road Linking Manto Sipi Cree Nation, Bunibonibee Cree Nation and God's Lake First Nation Public Notice Public Comments Invited on the Draft Environmental Assessment Report and Potential Conditions Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Nov 7, 2025 to Dec 8, 2025 2025-11-07 2025-12-08 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada November 7, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is conducting a federal environmental assessment of the proposed Project 6 - All-season Road Linking Manto Sipi Cree Nation, Bunibonibee Cree Nation, and God's Lake First Nation, located in Manitoba.

Share your thoughts about improving the resiliency of telecommunications networks and reliability of telecommunications services Closed Industry | Regulations | Science and technology Sept 4, 2025 to Dec 3, 2025 2025-09-04 2025-12-03 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission We are seeking your comments on new requirements and standards for how service providers design and operate their networks.
Share your thoughts: Options for emerald ash borer management in Canada Closed Agriculture | Economy | Plants Sept 29, 2025 to Nov 30, 2025 2025-09-29 2025-11-30 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The emerald ash borer (Agrilus planipennis) is a highly destructive invasive beetle that attacks and kills all species of ash (Fraxinus spp). It has already killed millions of trees in North America. The emerald ash borer is most commonly spread through the movement of firewood and other infested ash wood products, although it can also spread by flying.
Consultation on rectified clove leaf oil and DECCO 070 EC, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-13 Closed Food and drug | Health Oct 28, 2025 to Nov 27, 2025 2025-10-28 2025-11-27 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Clove Leaf Oil Technical and DECCO 070 EC, belonging to DECCO U.S. Post-Harvest, Inc., containing the technical grade active ingredient rectified clove leaf oil, to control potato sprouting.
Consultation on cyclobutrifluram, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-19 Closed Food and drug | Health | Policy Sept 12, 2025 to Nov 26, 2025 2025-09-12 2025-11-26 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for cyclobutrifluram up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 26 November 2025).
Consultation on fluoxapiprolin, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-20 Closed Food and drug | Health | Policy Sept 12, 2025 to Nov 26, 2025 2025-09-12 2025-11-26 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for fluoxapiprolin up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 26 November 2025).
Consultation document on Phase 1 PFAS prohibition: Risk management of PFAS, excluding fluoropolymers, not currently regulated in firefighting foams Closed Environment Sept 26, 2025 to Nov 25, 2025 2025-09-26 2025-11-25 Environment and Climate Change Canada The purpose of this consultation is to inform and solicit comments from stakeholders on risk management of PFAS, excluding fluoropolymers, not currently regulated in firefighting foams
Consultation Document on Phase 1 of the Risk Management of Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS), Excluding Fluoropolymers: Prohibition of the Use of PFAS, not Currently Regulated, in Firefighting Foams Closed Environment | Health | Regulations | Science and technology Sept 26, 2025 to Nov 25, 2025 2025-09-26 2025-11-25 Environment and Climate Change Canada The federal government, through Environment and Climate Change Canada and Health Canada, is asking Canadians, industry, non-governmental organizations, and Indigenous Peoples to share their ideas and suggestions, through email, mail and online submission. Comments will be considered in the development of proposed regulations that aim to completely phase out of the use of PFAS in firefighting foams to protect human health and the environment.
Consultation for guidance on notifying Health Canada of foreign actions Closed Health Sept 25, 2025 to Nov 24, 2025 2025-09-25 2025-11-24 Health Canada We invite stakeholders to provide their comments on the draft guidance document and notification forms. We will consider all input and feedback received before publishing the final versions. The update aims to address gaps and clarify issues identified in the existing guidance document. The update will also incorporate efficiencies associated with follow-up actions related to reporting, along with plain-language edits. The expected benefits of these updates include improved clarity and efficiencies for both the industry and Health Canada.

Share your thoughts: Proposed changes to the Chronic Wasting Disease Control Program Closed Agriculture | Animals | Health | Industry | Policy Sept 19, 2025 to Nov 21, 2025 2025-09-19 2025-11-21 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The CFIA is seeking feedback on proposed changes to its Chronic Wasting Disease (CWD) control program for farmed cervids.
Share your thoughts about improving the National Public Alerting System Closed Public safety July 15, 2025 to Nov 21, 2025 2025-07-15 2025-11-21 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission The National Public Alerting System (NPAS) is used by emergency management officials across Canada to warn the public about emergency situations like severe weather events and other potential concerns to public safety. This system is a shared responsibility between federal, provincial, and territorial governments and agencies. The CRTC plays a supporting role by requiring cellphone, cable and satellite television providers, and radio and television broadcasters to distribute emergency alerts to the public. Through this consultation, the CRTC is reviewing how public alerts are distributed in Canada. The goal is to help improve the NPAS, including by improving the accessibility of alerts, considering whether alerts should be distributed in languages that reflect local communities, and ensuring that they are available across the country.
Amendments to the Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) Concentration Limits for Automotive Refinishing Regulations- Consultation Document Closed Environment | Health | Industry | Regulations Sept 22, 2025 to Nov 21, 2025 2025-09-22 2025-11-21 Environment and Climate Change Canada To inform stakeholders of potential amendments to the Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) Concentration Limits for Automotive Refinishing Products Regulations (the Regulations) and to invite them to submit comments on the associated proposals. Additionally, this consultation document shares the results of the internal review of the Regulations and provides an opportunity for the public to provide feedback on how the Regulations could better support innovation, reduce administrative burden, and encourage the development and use of new technologies or best practices.
Share your thoughts: Proposed amendments to the Susceptible species of aquatic animals list - Perkinsus olseni and Perkinsus marinus Closed Agriculture | Animals | Economy | Exporting/Importing | Foreign affairs | Fisheries | Industry | International | Regulations | Trade Sept 19, 2025 to Nov 18, 2025 2025-09-19 2025-11-18 Canadian Food Inspection Agency CFIA is proposing to update the Susceptible Species of Aquatic Animals list (SSL), including adding or removing species and changes to disease susceptiblity profiles for species, in order to maintain alignment with the species listed by the World Organization for Animsl Health (WOAH). This specific proposal is to align CFIA's SSL with WOAH’s list of susceptible species for two diseases, Perkinsus marius and Perkinsus olseni.
Consultation on 3-Chloro-4-methylaniline hydrochloride and DRC-1339, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-12 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Oct 17, 2025 to Nov 16, 2025 2025-10-17 2025-11-16 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of DRC-1339 Technical and DRC-1339, containing the active ingredient 3-chloro-4-methylaniline hydrochloride, for control of corvids in relation to greater sage-grouse recovery.
Lynn Lake Gold Project Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Oct 16, 2025 to Nov 15, 2025 2025-10-16 2025-11-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada October 16, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period on proposed amendments to the decision statement for the Lynn Lake Gold Project, in Manitoba.
Creating Canada’s 6th National Action Plan on Open Government Closed Policy | Private sector | Society | Science and technology Feb 6, 2025 to Nov 14, 2025 2025-02-06 2025-11-14 Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat The Government of Canada continually renews its approach to open government. We are now collecting insights from the public. These insights will help shape Canada’s next National Action Plan on Open Government (NAP). This is a chance for you to share your ideas and stories, and what matters to you most in open government by visiting the consultation page.
Share your thoughts about consumer protections in the event of a service outage or disruption Closed Communication | Policy | Regulations | Service Sept 4, 2025 to Nov 13, 2025 2025-09-04 2025-11-13 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission We are seeking your comments on additional consumer protections to help ensure Canadians have the information they need during an outage. These measures include ensuring customers receive meaningful and timely updates, as well as refunds or bill credits after an outage.
Consultation on isofetamid 400SC Fungicide, containing isofetamid, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-09 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Sept 26, 2025 to Nov 10, 2025 2025-09-26 2025-11-10 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Isofetamid 400SC Fungicide, containing the technical grade active ingredient isofetamid, to control botrytis grey mould (Botrytis cinerea) on greenhouse ornamentals. This evaluation was completed under the User Requested Minor Use Label Expansion program, which is a cooperative program between Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada and Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency and includes participation by sponsors group, manufacturers, and both provincial and federal governments.
Consultation on isocycloseram, VANECTO Cockroach Gel Bait, EQUENTO and A23128 ST, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-11 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Oct 9, 2025 to Nov 8, 2025 2025-10-09 2025-11-08 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), under the authority of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Isocycloseram Technical, VANECTO Cockroach Gel Bait, EQUENTO and A23128 ST, containing the active ingredient isocycloseram. VANECTO Cockroach Gel Bait is for the control of cockroaches in commercial, industrial and residential buildings and other listed structures. EQUENTO is a seed treatment product for the control of insect pests on wheat, oat, barley, rye and triticale. A23128 ST, also containing active ingredients difenoconazole, sedaxane, metalaxyl-M (and S-isomer) and fludioxonil, is another seed treatment product for the control of insect pests and the control or suppression of seed-borne and soil-borne diseases on wheat, oat, barley, rye and triticale.
Consultation: Draft guidance on the plain language labelling regulations for prescription drugs Closed Health Sept 25, 2025 to Nov 7, 2025 2025-09-25 2025-11-07 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking feedback on proposed revisions to the Questions and Answers: Plain Language Labelling for Prescription Drugs Guidance Document.
Consultation on proposed updates to the danger to human health or safety assessment for free-standing clothing storage units Closed Regulations Oct 8, 2025 to Nov 7, 2025 2025-10-08 2025-11-07 Health Canada Health Canada invites stakeholders to provide comments on this updated danger to human health or safety assessment by consulting the assessment that has been posted to Table 2. Health Canada will consider these comments when making a determination on whether there is a danger to human health or safety posed by these products.
Canadian Marine Advisory Council - Fall 2025 Planned Transportation Nov 4, 2025 to Nov 6, 2025 2025-11-04 2025-11-06 Transport Canada Opportunity to attend meetings of the Canadian Marine Advisory Council (CMAC) and share views on marine safety and security.
Consulting Canadians on the operation of the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA) Closed Trade Sept 19, 2025 to Nov 3, 2025 2025-09-19 2025-11-03 Global Affairs Canada Consulting Canadians to seek their views and experiences on key areas of CUSMA that are working well and on potential areas for improvement. Submissions received from these consultations will help to inform Canada’s preparations for the joint review in 2026 and other efforts to ensure the effective operation of the Agreement. What We Heard Report available

Consultation on the Channel Darter (St. Lawrence River populations) management plan under the Species at Risk Act Planned Environment | Fisheries Oct 1, 2025 to Nov 1, 2025 2025-10-01 2025-11-01 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Fisheries and Oceans Canada will publish the draft management plan online to seek public comments.
Consultation on metamitron, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-15 Closed Food and drug | Health Aug 18, 2025 to Nov 1, 2025 2025-08-18 2025-11-01 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for metamitron up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by November 1, 2025).
Consultation on dicamba and its associated end-use products, Proposed Special Review Decision PSRD2025-01 Closed Food and drug | Health | Policy Sept 17, 2025 to Nov 1, 2025 2025-09-17 2025-11-01 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting the public and seeking your feedback on the proposed special review decision for dicamba and its associated end-use products. The consultation focuses specifically on the aspect of concern that initiated this special review: the potential risk to non-target terrestrial plants from the use of commercial dicamba products. Following the thorough environmental risk assessment for dicamba and a review of incident reports, Health Canada is proposing these changes to reduce the risk to non-target terrestrial plants: cancel one use, update spray drift buffer zones, add risk mitigation measures for volatility. You can find more details in the proposed special review decision document, including the scientific rationale and proposed changes.
Artificial Intelligence Strategy Closed Copyright/Trademarks/Patents | Economy | Economic development | Education | Foreign affairs | Human resources | Private sector | Public safety | Science and technology | Trade | Training and careers Oct 1, 2025 to Nov 1, 2025 2025-10-01 2025-11-01 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada is conducting a 30-day consultation to shape a renewed Artificial Intelligence Strategy (2026–2030) aimed at reinforcing Canada as a global leader in responsible and secure AI. This engagement seeks input on priorities for research, commercialization, adoption, and governance to ensure AI drives economic growth, safeguards rights, and builds public trust. Participants will help refine strategic goals, identify barriers, and propose actions that foster innovation, inclusivity, and ethical AI deployment across all sectors.
Consultation on the use of the customs broker business number (BN) following importer of record regulatory legislative changes Closed Economy | Exporting/Importing | Finance | Industry | International | Policy | Regulations | Trade Oct 1, 2025 to Oct 31, 2025 2025-10-01 2025-10-31 Canada Border Services Agency The Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) is seeking feedback on new scenarios in which customs brokers would be authorized to use their business number (BN) to submit accounting declarations on behalf of importers who are not yet registered in the CBSA Assessment and Revenue Management (CARM) system. What We Heard Report available
Volume-Related Composite Price Index Consultation, 2025 Closed Transportation July 10, 2025 to Oct 31, 2025 2025-07-10 2025-10-31 Canadian Transportation Agency The Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA) launched consultations to review the forecasting models used in determining the Volume-Related Composite Price Index (VRCPI).
Consultation: Guidance document updates for Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (GVP) inspections Closed Food and drug | Health Sept 15, 2025 to Oct 30, 2025 2025-09-15 2025-10-30 Health Canada Health Canada is announcing a 45-day consultation on the drafts of the updated GVP guidance documents. After consultation, the drafts will be finalized and replace the versions previously published in 2013.
Call for Housing Solutions on Public Land Bank Closed Housing July 2, 2025 to Oct 30, 2025 2025-07-02 2025-10-30 Public Services and Procurement Canada The federal government and provincial and territorial governments across Canada are sitting on surplus, underused, and vacant public lands, such as empty office towers or low-rise buildings. By unlocking more public lands for housing, the federal government can lower the costs of construction and build more homes, faster, at prices Canadians can afford. Though a previous version of the survey was distributed in Summer 2024, this version would allow for more precise questions related to lands, development, and housing. The purpose of this consultation is to explore ways to best support the government’s efforts through feedback from stakeholders, partners, and rights holders to build more homes on public lands. The consultation will also solicit feedback on the Public Land Bank geospatial tool to inform possible future iterations. The findings will be used to inform plans related to disposals of specific properties and broader private interest in land development. It will also assist in implementation strategy, allowing the team to gauge potential proponents and partners and potential hurdles in various sectors and jurisdictions.
Consultation on potential amendments to the Cannabis Tracking System Order Closed Food and drug | Health Aug 30, 2025 to Oct 29, 2025 2025-08-30 2025-10-29 Health Canada Health Canada invites you to comment on potential amendments to the Cannabis Tracking System Order (the Order). These changes would support the Government of Canada's goal to reduce regulatory red tape while continuing to monitor the high-level movement of cannabis through the supply chain.
Consultation: Classification of topical products containing human-derived exosomes, human extracellular vesicles, and/or human cell-conditioned media Closed Health Aug 14, 2025 to Oct 28, 2025 2025-08-14 2025-10-28 Health Canada The purpose of this consultation is to communicate to industry, practitioners, and the public about Health Canada's intended approach to the classification and regulation of topical products at the cosmetic-drug interface that contain: human-derived exosomes, human extracellular vesicles, and/or human cell-conditioned media. We are inviting comments, along with any other relevant information for consideration, from: manufacturers, distributors, other stakeholders.
Proposed Regulations Amending the Wildlife Area Regulations – Repeal of the Hunt Program and Entrance Fees in the Cap Tourmente National Wildlife Area Closed Amendments | Environment | Regulations | Recreation Sept 27, 2025 to Oct 27, 2025 2025-09-27 2025-10-27 Environment and Climate Change Canada Environment and Climate Change Canada is seeking comments on the proposed amendments to the Wildlife Area Regulations (the Regulations) for the Cap Tourmente National Wildlife Area (NWA) to: eliminate the migratory game bird hunt program, and eliminate the entrance and naturalist service fees
Consultation: Draft Nutrition for Healthy Term Infants (0 to 6 months) guidelines Closed Food and drug | Health Aug 19, 2025 to Oct 27, 2025 2025-08-19 2025-10-27 Health Canada The Nutrition for Healthy Term Infants guidelines help health professionals give consistent information on feeding infants, by providing: evidence-based recommendations; and a foundation for parent and caregiver resources. As part of our revision process, we're seeking comments on the draft guidelines for infant nutrition from birth to 6 months of age. Our objective is to ensure the final guidelines are clear, complete, relevant and useful.
Consultation on Trichoderma asperellum strain T34 and Asperello T34 Biocontrol, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-10 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Sept 26, 2025 to Oct 26, 2025 2025-09-26 2025-10-26 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of T34 Biocontrol Technical and Asperello T34 Biocontrol, belonging to Biocontrol Technologies, S.L., containing the active ingredient Trichoderma asperellum strain T34, a biological fungicide to be used against certain fungal diseases on outdoor terrestrial food and feed crops (potato, and field grown lettuce, strawberry and raspberry).
Breakwater Extension at St-Georges-de-Malbay Closed Environment Sept 15, 2025 to Oct 24, 2025 2025-09-15 2025-10-24 Fisheries and Oceans Canada The Small Craft Harbours Branch of Fisheries and Oceans Canada would like to invite comments, questions or concerns from the public regarding this project.
Share your thoughts: Proposed changes to the Common Names for Ingredients and Components Document for milk ingredients and modified milk ingredients Closed Advertising/Marketing | Food and drug | Industry | Policy Aug 22, 2025 to Oct 21, 2025 2025-08-22 2025-10-21 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is seeking feedback on proposed changes to the Common Names for Ingredients and Components Document that is incorporated by reference in the Food and Drug Regulations. Specifically, changes relating to the ingredients or components associated with the common names “milk ingredients” or “modified milk ingredients” that may appear on a food label. Certain ingredients or components, may be shown collectively in the list of ingredients using an optional name for the class of foods they belong to, instead of their individual common names. “Milk ingredients” and “modified milk ingredients” are two examples of these class common names. However, it’s not always clear which ingredients or components fall under these class common names. Additionally, the common names do not properly describe the nature of the ingredients and components that may be grouped under these class names.
ASC new user portal and public review feedback form Consultation Closed Communication | Persons with disabilities Sept 29, 2025 to Oct 20, 2025 2025-09-29 2025-10-20 Accessibility Standards Canada We held a consultation on our new user portal and feedback form for draft standards in line with the Accessible Canada Act’s objective of creating a barrier-free Canada by 2040 by improving accessibility in 7 priority areas, including information and communications technology and communications. The objectives were to test and gather feedback on: (1) the ease of accessing and using the user portal; (2) how well the feedback form supports participants in reviewing draft standards; (3) potential barriers or issues encountered when completing key tasks; and (4) ways to improve the clarity, usability, and accessibility of these tools. We invited individuals who are likely to use the portal and feedback form in different ways, including: (1) staff; (2) members of technical committees who review and draft accessibility standards; (3) representatives from the Canadian General Standards Board; and (4) staff from the Accessibility, Accommodation and Adaptive Computer Technology (AAACT) program. Participants were asked to complete a series of guided testing scenarios and to record whether each action was successful, providing comments and screenshots where issues occurred. Testing took place between September 29 and October 20, 2025. This consultation supports our ongoing efforts to enhance the accessibility, usability, and effectiveness of our digital tools. Insights gathered will inform future improvements to the portal and feedback form.
Have your say on the draft co-operation agreement between New Brunswick and Canada - Public comments invited Public notice Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Sept 19, 2025 to Oct 20, 2025 2025-09-19 2025-10-20 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada September 19, 2025 – Ottawa, ON – Impact Assessment Agency of Canada What is happening? The Government of Canada committed in May 2025 to striking co-operation agreements with every interested province within six months to realize its goal of “one project, one review”, while upholding Canada’s world-leading environmental standards and constitutional obligations to Indigenous Peoples.
“One Project, One Review”: Comments Invited on the Co-operation Approach for Working with Provinces Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Sept 19, 2025 to Oct 20, 2025 2025-09-19 2025-10-20 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada September 19, 2025 – Ottawa, ON – Impact Assessment Agency of Canada To meet the economic challenges of today and the future, the Government of Canada is focused on building one Canadian economy by expediting nation-building projects that will connect and transform the country. As part of this, the Government of Canada has committed to striking co-operation agreements with every interested province to realize its goal of "one project, one review" for major projects — a streamlined process where federal and provincial governments collaborate to meet their respective jurisdictional responsibilities to protect the environment and Indigenous rights through a single process.
Consultation on Amending the List of Species under the Species at Risk Act: Terrestrial Species January 2025 Open - accepting input Animals | Environment | Plants | Regulations Dec 10, 2024 to Oct 19, 2025 2024-12-10 2025-10-19 Environment and Climate Change Canada Annual consultation on the proposed modifications to the Schedule 1 of the Species at Risk Act.
Share your thoughts: Proposed amended livestock feed ingredients – Yeast-based ingredients Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Sept 17, 2025 to Oct 18, 2025 2025-09-17 2025-10-18 Canadian Food Inspection Agency We are seeking comments on the following amended single ingredient feed (SIF) descriptions: chromium yeast dehydrated (chromium enriched yeast); seleno yeast dehydrated (selenium enriched yeast). In addition, we are seeking comments on the following reclassified and amended SIF descriptions: yeast irradiated dehydrated; yeast brewers dehydrated; yeast brewers liquid; yeast torula dehydrated.
Public consultation on proposed regulatory changes in Canada Gazette Part I amending the Plant Breeders' Rights Regulations Closed Amendments | Copyright/Trademarks/Patents | Economic development | Plants | Regulations Aug 9, 2025 to Oct 18, 2025 2025-08-09 2025-10-18 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency is seeking feedback on proposed amendments to the Plant Breeders' Rights Regulations that will be posted in Canada Gazette Part I (CGI). The proposed amendments would address weaknesses in the current intellectual property (IP) regime, and support a business environment that attracts investment and innovation in plant breeding. Amendments would also align the regulations with 2015 legislative amendments to the Plant Breeders’ Rights Act (the Act) and resolve other administrative issues.

NPA 2025-005 - Simultaneous Independent Parallel Instrument Approaches – Closely Spaced Parallel Operations (CSPO) / Precision Runway Monitor (PRM) Open - accepting input Transportation Sept 24, 2025 to Oct 14, 2025 2025-09-24 2025-10-14 Transport Canada This Notice of Proposed Amendment (NPA) introduces standard amendments to align Canada’s authorization framework for Closely Spaced Parallel Operations (CSPO), also referred to as Precision Runway Monitor (PRM) operations, with international standards set out by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). The purpose of this initiative is to support the implementation of simultaneous independent parallel instrument approaches by NAV CANADA at Toronto Pearson International Airport (CYYZ), specifically targeting runways 15L/33R and 15R/33L. runways 15L/33R and 15R/33L.

Consultation on cyclobutrifluram, A22011 crop, A23156 crop, victrato and victrato 2, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-06 Closed Food and drug | Health | Policy Sept 12, 2025 to Oct 12, 2025 2025-09-12 2025-10-12 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of cyclobutrifluram Technical, A22011 Crop, A23156 Crop, VICTRATO and VICTRATO 2, containing the technical grade active ingredient cyclobutrifluram, a nematicide and fungicide for use on romaine lettuce and as a soybean seed treatment.
Consultation on Fluoxapiprolin and Xivana Prime, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-07 Closed Food and drug | Health | Policy Sept 12, 2025 to Oct 12, 2025 2025-09-12 2025-10-12 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Fluoxapiprolin 95 TC (the technical grade active ingredient) and Xivana Prime (the end-use product), containing the active ingredient fluoxapiprolin, for the control of late blight on potatoes, and against downy mildew and certain Phytophthora diseases on brassica head and stem vegetables, bulb vegetables, cucurbit vegetables, fruiting vegetables, leafy vegetables, leafy petiole vegetables, grapes and Amur river grapes.
Consultation on heptyl butyrate, disposable yellowjacket trap-W, yellowjacket trap cartridge refill, and yellowjacket trap, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-08 Closed Food and drug | Health | Policy Sept 12, 2025 to Oct 12, 2025 2025-09-12 2025-10-12 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), under the authority of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Rescue Yellowjacket Technical, Disposable Yellowjacket Trap-W, Yellowjacket Trap Cartridge Refill, and Yellowjacket Trap, containing the technical grade active ingredient heptyl butyrate, to control yellowjackets in urban and rural residential and recreational areas.
20th Conference of the Parties to the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species Closed Amendments | Animals | Economy | Environment | Exporting/Importing | Foreign affairs | Fisheries | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | International | Natural resources | Plants | Policy | Private sector | Regulations | Trade May 23, 2025 to Oct 10, 2025 2025-05-23 2025-10-10 Environment and Climate Change Canada The purpose of this consultation is to allow interested people in Canada to share views and provide input to inform Canada’s preparations and positions for the 20th Conference of the Parties to the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CoP20) that will be held in Samarkand, Uzbekistan, from November 24th to December 5th, 2025.
Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Supplemental Ingredients to permit 30% juice in certain caffeinated beverages Closed Food and drug | Health July 25, 2025 to Oct 8, 2025 2025-07-25 2025-10-08 Health Canada Anyone wishing to submit comments on the Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Supplemental Ingredients to permit 30% juice in certain caffeinated beverages may do so by sending an email to the Food and Nutrition Directorate's Food Incorporation by Reference Unit at food.ibr-ipr.aliments@hc-sc.gc.ca by October 8, 2025, 75 days from the date of this posting. Please use the words "juice-caffeine (P-SIS-25-02)" in the subject line of the email.

Share your thoughts: Future-proofing Canada’s Seeds Regulations for a stronger tomorrow: Proposals to modernize Canada's seed regulatory framework Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Exporting/Importing | Plants | Regulations | Science and technology July 29, 2025 to Oct 3, 2025 2025-07-29 2025-10-03 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The purpose of this consultation is to inform stakeholders on the Canadian Food Inspection Agency’s (CFIA’s) direction to modernize Parts I, III and IV of the Seeds Regulations. The CFIA will consider the feedback before publishing the draft amendments to the Seeds Regulations in Canada Gazette, Part I (CGI) for comment. The CFIA is looking to update the Seeds Regulations to: • improve responsiveness and consistency • reduce complexity • become adaptable and flexible to address future technical advances and scientific innovation • protect producers and consumers by strengthening existing requirements
IPPC Member consultation on draft International Standards for Phytosanitary Measures and specifications Closed Agriculture | Exporting/Importing | Plants July 1, 2025 to Sept 30, 2025 2025-07-01 2025-09-30 Canadian Food Inspection Agency This is an invitation to provide comments on the draft International Standards for Phytosanitary Measures (ISPMs), specifications for ISPMsrecently distributed for country consultation by the Secretariat of the International Plant Protection Convention (IPPC).
Deposit Insurance Review Closed Finance Aug 9, 2025 to Sept 26, 2025 2025-08-09 2025-09-26 Department of Finance Canada The government has been undertaking a review of the federal deposit insurance framework, including a public consultation process. This work is being led by the Department of Finance Canada, in collaboration with the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation and other financial sector agencies. This consultation seeks views and feedback on what changes to the deposit insurance framework, if any, are necessary to best support the evolving needs of Canadians and uphold financial stability.
National Capital (NCR) Residency for judges of the Federal Court and Tax Court of Canada Planned Justice and the Law Aug 5, 2025 to Sept 26, 2025 2025-08-05 2025-09-26 Department of Justice Canada Whether to revise the statutory requirement that the judges of the Federal Court and Tax Court of Canada reside in the NCR. Consultations will be targeted to sitting judges of affected courts; entities that support the federal courts, such as the Courts Administration Service; as well as entities representing lawyers, including associations representing lawyers from equity seeking groups, with the goal of better understanding the policy implications of any change to the current statutory requirement that the judges of the Federal Court and Tax Court of Canada reside in the NCR. Consultation will be in writing, based on a paper prepared by Justice Canada, with the possibility of in-person discussions.
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH Guideline E21: Inclusion of Pregnant and Breastfeeding Individuals in Clinical Trials Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to Sept 25, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-09-25 Health Canada The E21 Concept Paper in June 2023 the E21 Guideline seeks to provide a globally accepted framework and best practices to enable inclusion and/or retention of pregnant and breast-feeding individuals in clinical trials and will cover principles and practices to enable the collection of a sufficiently robust set of safety, efficacy, and/or pharmacokinetic data in pregnant and breast-feeding individuals which will better inform clinical decision-making in medicinal product use (e.g., improved product labelling). The guideline will establish a common understanding between regulatory authorities, industry, and other stakeholders and harmonise strategies and methodologies for enrollment and retention of pregnant and/or breast-feeding individuals into clinical trials and overall drug development plans.
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH Guideline E20: Adaptive Clinical Trials Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to Sept 25, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-09-25 Health Canada The E20 EWG is working on the development of a new E20 Guideline on “Adaptive Clinical Trials” on the design, conduct, analysis, and interpretation of adaptive clinical trials that provides a transparent and harmonized set of principles for the regulatory review of these studies in a global drug development program. These principles should also provide the flexibility to evaluate / discuss innovative approaches to clinical trial design throughout the development process.
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH Guideline M4Q(R2): Common Technical Document (CTD)-Quality Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to Sept 25, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-09-25 Health Canada The M4Q(R2) EWG is focusing on the revision of CTD Quality sections in Modules 2 and 3 to capture quality information for the registration and lifecycle management of pharmaceuticals for human use. The main issues to be resolved during this revision include expanding the scope of M4Q(R1) guideline This M4Q(R2) guideline applies to all pharmaceutical drug substances and products (both chemical and biological) that require a marketing authorization ; establishing the role of M4Q(R2) as the main source of the structure and location of regulatory quality information; organizing product and manufacturing information in a suitable format for easy access, analysis, and knowledge management; incorporating concepts and data expectations presented in ICH Quality guidelines and aligning with currently recognized international standards and guidelines; better capturing the pharmaceutical development and the proposed overall control strategy, which should be the backbone of the revised M4Q structure. This should address key elements of the proposed pharmaceutical product, including the Quality Target Product Profile (QTPP), manufacturing process, and overall control strategy; enhancing the Quality Module 2 to facilitate the efficiency and effectiveness of regulatory submissions and assessments.
Regional Assessment of Offshore Oil and Gas Exploratory Drilling East of Newfoundland and Labrador Public Notice Public Comments Invited on the Draft Follow-up Program Report for 2024-2025 Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Aug 25, 2025 to Sept 24, 2025 2025-08-25 2025-09-24 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada August 25, 2025 — The follow-up program for the Regional Assessment (RA) of Offshore Oil and Gas Exploratory Drilling East of Newfoundland and Labrador, tracks and reports on the progress of the commitments outlined in the Ministerial Response to the Regional Assessment Committee's Report.
Consultation on metamitron, Brevis 150 SC, and Brevis 15 SG, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-05 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Aug 8, 2025 to Sept 22, 2025 2025-08-08 2025-09-22 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of ADAMA Metamitron Technical, Brevis 150 SC, and Brevis 15 SG, containing the active ingredient metamitron, for thinning of apples and pears.
Engagement on renewing Canada’s National Financial Literacy Strategy Open - accepting input Economy | Finance Sept 12, 2025 to Sept 19, 2025 2025-09-12 2025-09-19 Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Financial Consumer Agency of Canada is engaging with stakeholders to both help assess the impact of the current National Strategy and to inform a future renewal.
Woodfibre LNG Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited on Proposed Amendments to the Decision Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Sept 3, 2025 to Sept 18, 2025 2025-09-03 2025-09-18 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada September 3, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period on proposed amendments to the decision statement for the Woodfibre LNG Project, a natural gas liquefaction facility and marine terminal located seven kilometres southwest of Squamish, British Columbia on the northwestern shoreline of Howe Sound.

What We Heard Report available
Consultation on the proposed proposed Management plan for the Magdalen Islands Grasshopper (Melanoplus madeleineae) Closed Environment | Policy July 17, 2025 to Sept 15, 2025 2025-07-17 2025-09-15 Environment and Climate Change Canada Request comments from stakeholders
Yellowhead Copper Project Public Notice Comments Invited & Information Sessions on the Initial Project Description Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Aug 5, 2025 to Sept 15, 2025 2025-08-05 2025-09-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada August 5, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada and British Columbia’s Environmental Assessment Office invite Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description for the Yellowhead Copper Project and provide feedback by September 15, 2025. Comments received will be used to prepare the joint Summary of Issues and Engagement document.
Consultation on revised procedures for the registration of pesticides for emergency use, Regulatory Proposal PRO2025-03 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Regulations July 16, 2025 to Sept 14, 2025 2025-07-16 2025-09-14 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on Regulatory Proposal PRO2025-03, Consultation on Revised Procedures for the Registration of Pesticides for Emergency Use. PRO2025-03 contains proposed guidance for registering pesticides or amending registrations for emergency control of seriously detrimental pest infestations. Where currently registered pesticides and non-chemical control methods or practices are insufficient to address the pest outbreak, the PMRA will consider requests for registration of pesticides for emergency use. The information proposed in PRO2025-03 is intended to replace the current DIR2017-03, Registration of Pesticides for Emergency Use: Revised Procedures, dated 31 August 2017.
Share your thoughts: Proposed new livestock feed ingredients – Pea processing residues dehydrated and pea processing residues wet Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Aug 13, 2025 to Sept 12, 2025 2025-08-13 2025-09-12 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is seeking comments on the following 2 proposed new single ingredient feed (SIF) descriptions: - pea processing residues dehydrated - pea processing residues wet
Share your thoughts: Proposed new livestock feed ingredient – Pea solubles Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Aug 13, 2025 to Sept 12, 2025 2025-08-13 2025-09-12 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is seeking comments on the following proposed new single ingredient feed (SIF) description for pea solubles for a new purpose (as a pelleting aid). In addition, the CFIA is seeking comments on the amended description for pea solubles approved as a source of dietary energy and protein in feeds for cattle and swine.
Evaluation by Environment and Climate Change Canada of the ongoing need for the Emergency Order for the Protection of the Western Chorus Frog (La Prairie, Candiac, Saint-Philippe) Closed Environment June 15, 2024 to Sept 12, 2025 2024-06-15 2025-09-12 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the ongoing need for the emergency order protecting Western Chorus Frog in the municipalities of La Prairie, Candiac, and Saint-Philippe, Quebec.
Consultation on the third legislative review of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to Sept 12, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-09-12 Health Canada Health Canada is launching a public consultation to inform the third legislative review of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act (TVPA). This third Legislative Review of the TVPA, will focus on compliance and enforcement challenges including the potential need for additional or enhanced tools for a progressive enforcement approach. At this time we are seeking ideas, experiences and perspectives from Canadians, relevant stakeholders and partners. You are encouraged to submit any data that you may have to support your responses. All submissions will be reviewed and considered in the development of a final report, which will be tabled in Parliament.
Sorel-Tracy Port Terminal Project Public Notice Comments Invited on the Summary of the Impact Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Aug 12, 2025 to Sept 11, 2025 2025-08-12 2025-09-11 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada August 12, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is inviting comments by September 11, 2025, on the potential effects of the project and any proposed measures to prevent or mitigate those effects, as described in the proponent’s Impact Statement.
Yellowhead Copper Project Public notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Aug 12, 2025 to Sept 11, 2025 2025-08-12 2025-09-11 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada August 12, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is making funding available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for this project. Applications received by September 11, 2025, will be considered.
Share your thoughts on improving public interest participation Closed Communication | Financial assistance and entitlements | Justice and the Law | Policy May 12, 2025 to Sept 9, 2025 2025-05-12 2025-09-09 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission We are seeking your input on how the CRTC can better support public interest participation in its proceedings. The CRTC is an independent quasi-judicial tribunal responsible for regulating the Canadian communications sector in the public interest. It encourages people with a diversity of perspectives to participate in its proceedings. People that represent the public interest can apply for funding to cover some of their costs of participating in proceedings, for example to commission expert research, conduct surveys of Canadians, or receive legal advice. Through this public consultation, the CRTC aims to make funding more predictable and faster by simplifying the application process.
Consultation on Guidance Document: Information and Submission Requirements for Biosimilar Biologic Drugs Closed Health June 10, 2025 to Sept 8, 2025 2025-06-10 2025-09-08 Health Canada Health Canada is proposing revisions to its Guidance Document: Information and Submission Requirements for Biosimilar Biologic Drugs.
Consultation on potassium bicarbonate and its associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-06 Closed Food and drug | Health June 10, 2025 to Sept 8, 2025 2025-06-10 2025-09-08 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of products containing potassium bicarbonate registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed updates to standard label statements.
Consultation on Sulphur and its associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-07 Closed Food and drug | Health June 10, 2025 to Sept 8, 2025 2025-06-10 2025-09-08 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of products containing sulphur registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed label amendments including updated label statements and mitigation measures.
Consultation on Putrescent Whole Egg Solids, Indole, Sucrose, Trimethylamine, Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain Y243, and related end-use products, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-04 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health July 25, 2025 to Sept 8, 2025 2025-07-25 2025-09-08 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Rescue Egg Technical, Big Bag Indole Technical, Rescue Sucrose Technical, Rescue Trimethylamine Technical, Rescue Saccharomyces cerevisiae Technical, and related end-use products, Reusable Fly Trap, Fly Trap, Fly Trap MAX, POP! Fly Trap, and Big Bag Fly Trap, containing the active ingredients putrescent whole egg solids, indole, sucrose, trimethylamine, and Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain Y243, to attract and trap flies in urban and rural residential and recreational areas.
Canada-Alberta equivalency agreement respecting the release of methane from the upstream oil and gas sector Closed Environment | Health | Industry | Natural resources | Private sector | Regulations July 5, 2025 to Sept 3, 2025 2025-07-05 2025-09-03 Environment and Climate Change Canada To avoid creating regulatory overlap and administrative burdern for provincially regulated oil and gas operators, the draft equivalency Order and agreement were published on July 5, 2025 declaring that the Government of Alberta’s Methane Emission Reduction Regulation (MERR) and Directives 017 and 060 contain similar requirements to the Federal Methane Regulations and were determined to meet the criteria for entering into an equivalency agreement under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act (CEPA). Once the final equivalency Order and Agreement are finalized, the federal methane regulations will stand down in Alberta.
2025 consultations on economic priorities for category-based selection in Express Entry Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Immigration | Industry | Labour | Policy Aug 6, 2025 to Sept 3, 2025 2025-08-06 2025-09-03 Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) is consulting with stakeholders and partners, as per legislative requirements, on economic priorities and needs to inform categories for Express Entry, the online system that IRCC uses to manage immigration applications from skilled workers.
GL 712-1-1 Releases Under the Provisions of Sections 84 and 84.1 of the CCRA Closed Justice and the Law | Policy | Public safety Aug 11, 2025 to Sept 2, 2025 2025-08-11 2025-09-02 Correctional Service of Canada To engage internal and external stakeholders on the policy, and ask for their comments.
Greenlight Electricity Centre Project Public notice Public Comments Invited and Information Sessions on the Summary of the Initial Project Description Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Aug 11, 2025 to Sept 2, 2025 2025-08-11 2025-09-02 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada August 11, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is inviting Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description for the Greenlight Electricity Centre Project and provide feedback by September 2, 2025. Comments received will be used to prepare the summary of issues.
Predictable and Efficient Processing and Assessment of Negligible-Risk and Low-Risk Activities Closed Amendments | Environment | Industry | Public safety | Regulations July 9, 2024 to Aug 31, 2025 2024-07-09 2025-08-31 Canada Energy Regulator The Efficiency Project is exploring the assessment processes for negligible-risk and low-risk activities currently addressed by the Section 58 Streamlining Order, as well as for activities currently addressed by the Decommissioning Exemption Order. The intent of the Efficiency Project is to create criteria, processes, and filing guidance that capture activities that may be of negligible-risk or low-risk, such as those located within company-owned and fenced land. More specifically, the Efficiency Project involves development of the following: criteria for negligible-risk activities; simplified processes for negligible-risk and low-risk applications under section 214 of the CER Act, and negligible-risk decommissioning applications; and updated filing guidance. Through publishing this discussion paper, the CER is seeking comment on the draft qualifying criteria for negligible-risk and low-risk applications, the draft simplified processes for those applications under section 214 of the CER Act, and negligible-risk decommissioning applications. Updated filing guidance will be developed at a later date. What We Heard Report available
Consultation on linuron, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-07 Closed Food and drug | Health June 17, 2025 to Aug 31, 2025 2025-06-17 2025-08-31 Health Canada Under the authority of the Pest Control Products Act, Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) is proposing acceptability of the requested application to re-instate the use on the commodity of celery to the product label of Lorox L Herbicide containing technical grade linuron, for the control of labelled broadleaf and grassy weeds as an early postemergent foliar use.
Consultation on florylpicoxamid, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-08 Closed Food and drug | Health June 17, 2025 to Aug 31, 2025 2025-06-17 2025-08-31 Health Canada Under the authority of the Pest Control Products Act, Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) is proposing acceptability of the request to specify MRLs for florylpicoxamid on the imported commodities of bananas and mangoes to control Black Sigatoka, Pseudocercospora fijiensis.
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH Guideline M13B: Bioequivalence for Immediate-Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to Aug 31, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-08-31 Health Canada Part of the foreseen ICH M13 Guideline series (M13A-C), the ICH M13B Guideline is intended to provide recommendations on obtaining waivers of bioequivalence (BE) studies for one or more additional strengths of a drug product in an application where in vivo BE has been demonstrated for at least one of the strengths. This guideline is applicable to both development and post approval phases for orally administered immediate-release (IR) solid oral dosage forms designed to deliver drugs to the systemic circulation, such as tablets, capsules, and granules/powders for oral suspension. The ICH M13B Guideline is the second Guideline in the foreseen series to describe the scientific and technical aspects of demonstrating BE for additional strengths of a drug product, i.e., obtaining waiver(s) for one or more strengths in an application with multiple strengths when BE has been demonstrated for at least one of the strengths following ICH M13A. How regulatory decisions may be made based on BE assessment is out of the scope of this guideline.
Release of draft (step 2) ICH Guideline Q1: Stability Testing of Drug Substances and Drug Products Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to Aug 31, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-08-31 Health Canada The Q1/Q5C EWG was established to revise the current ICH Stability Guideline Series Q1A-F and Q5C by streamlining the series, combining the various guidelines into a single guideline focused on core stability principles; promoting harmonised interpretation by addressing potential gaps and areas of ambiguity; Addressing additional technical issues, including relevant stability strategies and innovative tools that strengthen the application of risk management; considering inclusion of new topics, such as stability considerations for advanced therapies. The envisioned result is a combined guideline, ICH Q1, with integrated annexes and/or appendices that address specific topics beyond the core stability recommendations and principles. Addressing drug product types including substances, intermediates, and devices.
Share your thoughts: Canadian Food Inspection Agency's 2026 to 2028 Accessibility Plan Closed Persons with disabilities July 31, 2025 to Aug 30, 2025 2025-07-31 2025-08-30 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The CFIA is developing its 2026 to 2028 Accessibility Plan. The plan will outline updated commitments to improve accessibility across the agency's policies, programs, services, workplaces and operations. It builds on the CFIA's 2023 to 2026 Accessibility Plan. This is an opportunity for people with disabilities, caregivers and stakeholders to share their experiences and identify barriers when interacting with CFIA programs, services or workplaces.
Income Tax Regulation 105 Waiver Process Closed Taxes June 30, 2025 to Aug 29, 2025 2025-06-30 2025-08-29 Canada Revenue Agency The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) is reviewing the Income Tax Regulation 105 waiver process. Under this regulation, every payer is required to withhold 15% from payments made to non-residents for services provided in Canada. The payers are required to withhold for the potential tax owing by non-residents. This withholding tax can be a significant burden, especially if it exceeds the actual tax liability.
Pre-Budget Consultations 2025 Closed Economy July 14, 2025 to Aug 28, 2025 2025-07-14 2025-08-28 Department of Finance Canada This fall, the Government of Canada will table Budget 2025, which will lay out the government's plan to address the unprecedented challenges facing our country. Budget 2025 will catalyze investment and economic growth across Canada to build one united Canadian economy – all while spending less on the day-to-day operations of government. Pre-budget consultations are an opportunity for Canadians from across the country to share their ideas and priorities on how to better shape Canada's future. Join the conversation by responding to our questionnaire below or sending in a formal submission both of which can be submitted until August 28, 2025.
Commissioner's Directive 581: Violations of the Law by Offenders and Procedures Manual - Violations of the Law by Offenders Closed Justice and the Law | Policy | Public safety Aug 1, 2025 to Aug 25, 2025 2025-08-01 2025-08-25 Correctional Service of Canada To engage internal and external stakeholders on the policy, and ask for their comments.
Commissioner's Directive 026: Communications Closed Justice and the Law | Policy | Public safety July 22, 2025 to Aug 25, 2025 2025-07-22 2025-08-25 Correctional Service of Canada To engage internal and external stakeholders on the policy, and ask for their comments.
Consulting Canadians on a possible Canada-European Union Digital Trade Agreement Closed International | Trade June 25, 2025 to Aug 25, 2025 2025-06-25 2025-08-25 Global Affairs Canada The Government of Canada has commenced work to launch exploratory discussions with the European Union for a Canada-EU digital trade agreement (DTA). Global Affairs Canada is seeking to inform Canada's position, interests, and sensitivities with respect to digital trade with the EU. As such, Global Affairs Canada invites all Canadians to share their views regarding the development of this Canada-EU DTA, including feedback on its potential scope, and views on the types of provisions that may be included. What We Heard Report available
Internal Liquidity Adequacy Assessment Process (ILAAP) discussion paper Closed Finance May 22, 2025 to Aug 22, 2025 2025-05-22 2025-08-22 Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Canada The Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions is holding a 90-day consultation on Pillar 2 liquidity and funding risk, with a discussion paper describing a more structured supervisory review process for liquidity risk in Canadian deposit-taking institutions, specifically, an ILAAP.
Josephburg Condensate Fractionation Project Public Notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society July 21, 2025 to Aug 20, 2025 2025-07-21 2025-08-20 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada July 21, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for the proposed Josephburg Condensate Fractionation Project, a new condensate distillation facility (refinery) in Alberta's Industrial Heartland, about 22 kilometres northeast of Edmonton, Alberta.
Final Minimum Capital Test (MCT) Guideline (2026) Closed Finance May 22, 2025 to Aug 20, 2025 2025-05-22 2025-08-20 Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Canada OSFI published the final Minimum Capital Test (MCT) Guideline following a 90-day public consultation held from May 22 to August 20, 2025.OSFI simplified the MCT guidance on insurance risk (specifically unexpired coverage) as well as updated regulatory adjustments to net assets available for insurance receivables and the capital confirmation requirements for user fees. What We Heard Report available
2025 consultations on immigration levels Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Education | Housing | Health | Immigration | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | International | Labour | Policy | Rural and remote services | Society | Youth July 21, 2025 to Aug 17, 2025 2025-07-21 2025-08-17 Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Every year, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) engages with a wide range of stakeholders and partners from across the country to help us shape Canada’s next Immigration Levels Plan. Traditionally, the Immigration Levels Plan focused on how many permanent residents would be admitted to Canada in a given year, and set targets for overall admissions per immigration category. Last year, we expanded that scope by including targets for temporary resident arrivals of students and workers. Building on last year’s new approach, this year will be guided by the Government of Canada’s commitments to lower the non-permanent resident population to less than 5% of Canada’s population by the end of 2027, stabilize permanent resident admissions at less than 1% of Canada’s population annually beyond 2027, and establish a 12% target for Francophone immigration outside of Quebec by 2029 to promote the vitality of Francophone communities. As part of our whole-of government, whole-of-society approach to immigration, we welcome the views of stakeholders and partners as we prepare for the 2026–2028 Immigration Levels Plan, which will be announced in the fall of 2025.
Troilus Mining Project Public Notice Comments Invited on the Summary of the Impact Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society July 3, 2025 to Aug 17, 2025 2025-07-03 2025-08-17 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada July 3, 2025 — The Joint Assessment Committee (the Joint Committee) comprising of representatives from the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) and the Cree Nation Government, is conducting a federal impact assessment for the proposed Troilus Mining Project, located approximately 76 kilometres northwest of the Cree community of Mistissini and approximately 170 kilometres north of Chibougamau, Quebec.
Consultation: Categorization of antimicrobial drugs based on importance in human medicine Closed Animals | Food and drug | Health June 18, 2025 to Aug 16, 2025 2025-06-18 2025-08-16 Health Canada Health Canada's Veterinary Drugs Directorate (VDD) is seeking comments on proposed revisions to its Categorization of Antimicrobial Drugs Based on Importance in Human Medicine.
Mont Sorcier Mining Project Public Notice Comments Invited on the Draft Tailored Impact Statement Guidelines and Draft Public Participation Plan Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society July 17, 2025 to Aug 16, 2025 2025-07-17 2025-08-16 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada July 17, 2025 — The Joint Assessment Committee (the Joint Committee), comprising of representatives from the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) and the Cree Nation Government, is conducting a federal impact assessment for the proposed Mont Sorcier Mining Project, a new open-pit iron and vanadium mine, located in the Eeyou Istchee James Bay Territory, in Quebec.
Adjusted consultation period for Proposed Registration Decision for Category A applications (Registration of new active ingredients or major new uses of pesticides), Notice of Intent NOI2025-02 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Policy | Regulations July 15, 2025 to Aug 15, 2025 2025-07-15 2025-08-15 Health Canada The Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on NOI2025-02, Notice of Intent – Adjusted consultation period for Proposed Registration Decision (PRD) for Category A applications (Registration of new active ingredients or major new uses of pesticides).
Centre Village Renewables Integration and Grid Security Project Public Notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society July 16, 2025 to Aug 15, 2025 2025-07-16 2025-08-15 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada July 16, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for the proposed Centre Village Renewables Integration and Grid Security Project, a new electricity generation facility, located in Centre Village, near Sackville in southeastern New Brunswick.
Bulletin on Private Access to the Competition Tribunal Closed Amendments | Industry | Justice and the Law | Private sector June 20, 2025 to Aug 15, 2025 2025-06-20 2025-08-15 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada In June 2025 the Competition Bureau published a revised information bulletin presenting its perspective on private access to the Competition Tribunal following recent changes to the Competition Act that expanded access. The amended guidance explains the Bureau’s views on private access to the Tribunal and clarifies when it may take certain actions that impact proceedings. Canadians are invited to submit their feedback by e-mail at cb.PAconsultation-bc.consultationPA@cb-bc.gc.ca by August 19, 2025.
Updated Proposal - Ministerial class exemption order for the exceptional importation and sale of certain foods for a special dietary purpose to mitigate a shortage or risk of a shortage Closed Food and drug June 30, 2025 to Aug 14, 2025 2025-06-30 2025-08-14 Health Canada To provide Canadians and interested stakeholders with the opportunity to comment on Health Canada revised proposal.

Consultation: Proposed Regulatory Amendments for Precursor Chemicals and Designated Devices Closed Health June 28, 2025 to Aug 12, 2025 2025-06-28 2025-08-12 Health Canada The purpose of this consultation is to inform interested parties of Health Canada's intent to advance amendments to the Precursor Control Regulations and Schedule IX to the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act. Interested parties are invited to submit comments for a 45-day period starting on June 28, 2025.
Changing the Saskatoon International Airport Zoning Regulations Closed Transportation June 11, 2025 to Aug 11, 2025 2025-06-11 2025-08-11 Transport Canada The zoning regulations for the Saskatoon International Airport have been in place for 37 years. Transport Canada wants to change these regulations so that the airport can move ahead with long-term planning, and add additional protection for flight crews, aircraft passengers, and members of nearby communities. We also want to add measures to make bird strikes less likely and make it harder for structures to interfere with aircraft communications.
Share your thoughts: Proposed amended livestock feed ingredients – Miscellaneous changes to the Canadian Feed Ingredients Table Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations July 9, 2025 to Aug 8, 2025 2025-07-09 2025-08-08 Canadian Food Inspection Agency We are seeking comments on the following amended single ingredient feed (SIF) descriptions: fresh liquid whey; calcium iodate; hydrolyzed chicken feathers and blood meal; Trichoderma longibrachiatum fermentation extract condensed; Trichoderma longibrachiatum fermentation extract dehydrated; Trichoderma longibrachiatum fermentation solubles dehydrated; Trichoderma longibrachiatum fermentation solubles condensed; camelina meal mechanical extracted; cattle buttermilk dehydrated; cattle milk dehydrated; cattle skim milk dehydrated; cattle whey protein dehydrated; cattle whey dehydrated; calcium oxide
