In addition, we are seeking comments on the amended descriptions for eugenol and vanillin approved for use as flavour ingredients
Josephburg Condensate Fractionation Project - Public comments and information sessions on the summary of the initial project description Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society July 17, 2025 to Aug 6, 2025 2025-07-17 2025-08-06 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada July 17, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada is inviting Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description for the Josephburg Condensate Fractionation Project and provide feedback by August 6, 2025. Comments received will be used to prepare the summary of issues.
Discussion paper DIS-25-01, Regulatory Readiness for Fusion Closed Regulations June 5, 2024 to Aug 4, 2025 2024-06-05 2025-08-04 Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission The purpose of this discussion paper is to outline the CNSC’s conceptual framework for regulating fusion energy and technology, and to consult on this framework with industry, Indigenous Nations and communities, academic institutions, members of the public and other interested parties.
Consultation on the implementation of the Global Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR) Forum certifications in Canada Closed Industry | International | Policy | Private sector | Trade Mar 7, 2025 to July 31, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-07-31 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada Innovation, Science and Economic Development (ISED) is conducting this consultation to inform its approach to implementing the Global Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR) Forum certifications. ISED is seeking comments from organizations regulated by the Personal Information Protection and Electronics Document Act (PIPEDA) or substantially similar legislation, organizations interested in playing a role as a certification body, and any other interested parties.
Alexander Graham Bell National Historic Site Management Plan Open - accepting input Heritage June 23, 2025 to July 31, 2025 2025-06-23 2025-07-31 Parks Canada Parks Canada is holding public consultation for the draft management plan for the Alexander Graham Bell National Historic Site. Parks Canada will be engaging community stakeholders, Canadians and visitors to help guide future activities at the site through email, online engagement and meetings.
REGDOC-2.6.4, Chemistry Control for Reactor Facilities Closed Regulations July 15, 2025 to July 28, 2025 2025-07-15 2025-07-28 Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission Regulatory document REGDOC-2.6.4, Chemistry Control for Reactor Facilities, clarifies the requirements for, and provides guidance on, developing, implementing and maintaining a chemistry control program for a reactor facility. This regulatory document applies to new and existing reactor facilities, including nuclear power plants (NPPs), advanced reactors, small modular reactors (SMRs), and research reactors.
Construction of a sediment storage area at the Millerand fishing harbour in the Magdalen Islands Closed Environment Nov 15, 2024 to July 25, 2025 2024-11-15 2025-07-25 Fisheries and Oceans Canada The Small Craft Harbours Branch of Fisheries and Oceans Canada would like to invite comments, questions or concerns from the public regarding this project. The project includes constructing a sediment storage area to allow for their reclamation. Work on Pier 401 is also planned to restore the structure.
Public consultation on the principle of dependency under the Madrid System Closed Amendments | Copyright/Trademarks/Patents | International | Policy June 10, 2025 to July 25, 2025 2025-06-10 2025-07-25 Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada This consultation pertains to the principle of dependency under the Madrid System as it is currently implemented in Canada and the Registrar of Trademarks is seeking comments in relation to the potential changes that are being proposed at the Working Group on the Legal Development of the Madrid System for the International Registration of Marks.
Peace River Nuclear Power Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society June 23, 2025 to July 23, 2025 2025-06-23 2025-07-23 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada June 23, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) and the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission (CNSC) are conducting an integrated assessment for the proposed Peace River Nuclear Power Project, a new nuclear power plant located north of the Town of Peace River, Alberta.
Draft Liquidity Adequacy Requirements (LAR) Guideline Closed Finance May 22, 2025 to July 22, 2025 2025-05-22 2025-07-22 Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Canada OSFI is beginning a 60-day consultation on proposed changes to the LAR Guideline. This includes proposed new funding categories for deposits involving non-bank financial intermediaries and for products such as structured notes.
Regional Information and Monitoring Network (RIMNet) for the Ottawa River Watershed Closed Regulations June 19, 2024 to July 21, 2025 2024-06-19 2025-07-21 Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission The Regional Information and Monitoring Network (RIMNet) is an initiative by the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission (CNSC) and Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC) to enhance information sharing and document the environmental aspects of nuclear facilities and activities in the Ottawa River / Kichi Sibi watershed. By centralizing resources, RIMNet aims to increase transparency, build public confidence and foster trust. It is independent and not linked to any specific nuclear project or activity.
Regional Assessment in the Ring of Fire Area Public Notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society June 19, 2025 to July 21, 2025 2025-06-19 2025-07-21 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada June 19, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the conduct phase of the co-led Regional Assessment in the Ring of Fire Area. The Ring of Fire area is located about 540 kilometres northeast of Thunder Bay, Ontario.
CCHCC consultation on draft policy direction for Embodied Greenhouse Gas Emissions in the National Model Codes Closed Environment | Housing | Regulations | Science and technology May 30, 2025 to July 21, 2025 2025-05-30 2025-07-21 National Research Council Canada The CBHCC’s work plan for the 2030 code cycle includes the development of technical requirements to minimize embodied GHG emissions. As such, input is requested on a draft policy position paper for addressing embodied greenhouse gas emissions in new houses and buildings in the 2030 National Model Codes.
Consultation of the Interim Management Plan for Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area Closed Development | Environment | Natural resources June 23, 2025 to July 21, 2025 2025-06-23 2025-07-21 Parks Canada To share the draft Interim Management Plan with the Canadian public and hear what they have to say.
Share your thoughts: Participate in the risk assessment process for four lines of Gene Edited Pigs Closed Environment | Health | Science and technology June 20, 2025 to July 20, 2025 2025-06-20 2025-07-20 Environment and Climate Change Canada The federal government, through Environment and Climate Change Canada and Health Canada, is asking Canadians, industry, non-governmental organizations, and Indigenous Peoples to share their ideas and suggestions, through email, mail, workshops and online engagement. Feedback will help develop a renewed federal chemicals management program to protect human health and the environment.
Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project Public Notice Comments Invited on the Draft Tailored Impact Statement Guidelines and Draft Public Participation Plan Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society June 19, 2025 to July 19, 2025 2025-06-19 2025-07-19 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada June 19, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is conducting an impact assessment for the proposed Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project, a new open-pit and underground lithium mine located east of Radisson, Quebec.
Canada Revenue Agency 2025 consultations on accessibility Closed Taxes June 17, 2025 to July 18, 2025 2025-06-17 2025-07-18 Canada Revenue Agency The CRA consulted with organizations that support or serve persons with disabilities. Participants provided valuable insights on accessibility barriers and suggested improvements across the CRA’s programs and services. The input the CRA gathered through this exercise will help shape its 2026-2028 Accessibility Plan. 
Consultation on Wiinipaakw Indigenous Protected Area and National Marine Conservation Area Closed Children | Indigenous Peoples | Rural and remote services | Youth June 11, 2025 to July 17, 2025 2025-06-11 2025-07-17 Parks Canada Parks Canada and the Cree Nation Government are pleased to be reaching out to Canadians in order to understand their views related to the proposed Wiinipaakw Indigenous Protected Area (IPA) and National Marine Conservation Area (NMCA). This proposed protected area is located in the Eeyou Marine Region, in offshore areas of Eastern James Bay. This web survey is intended for both Indigenous and non-Indigenous individuals who are interested in commenting. This survey complements other engagement and consultation activities, including in-person meetings on this initiative.
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH M11: Clinical electronic Structured Harmonised Protocol (CeSHarP) – Technical Specification Closed Food and drug June 27, 2025 to July 15, 2025 2025-06-27 2025-07-15 Health Canada Purpose is to seek feedback from stakeholders on the new draft ICH M11 Technical Specification, a new guideline on Clinical Electronic Structured Harmonized Protocol (CeSHarP).
Main Breakwater Repair at Rivière-au-Renard Harbour Closed Environment Mar 14, 2025 to July 14, 2025 2025-03-14 2025-07-14 Fisheries and Oceans Canada The Small Craft Harbours Branch of Fisheries and Oceans Canada would like to invite comments, questions or concerns from the public regarding this project. This project provides for the work necessary to access and repair the main breakwater at Rivière-au-Renard Harbour, whose current state no longer allows it to fulfill its functions.
Obadjiwan-Fort-Témiscamingue National Historic Site Draft Management Plan, 2025 Closed Heritage | Indigenous Peoples | Society | Youth June 14, 2025 to July 14, 2025 2025-06-14 2025-07-14 Parks Canada The objective of this consultation is to enable the general public to participate in decision-making concerning the management of the national historic site.

Development of a service area - Kegaska Fishing Harbour - Basse-Côte-Nord Closed Environment June 10, 2025 to July 11, 2025 2025-06-10 2025-07-11 Fisheries and Oceans Canada The Small Craft Harbours Branch of Fisheries and Oceans Canada would like to invite comments, questions or concerns from the public regarding this project. The project includes the redevelopment and expansion of the service area to meet the needs of users.
Consultation on tea tree oil, Timorex Gold, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-03 Closed Food and drug | Health June 11, 2025 to July 11, 2025 2025-06-11 2025-07-11 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), under the authority of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Timorex Gold, containing the technical grade active ingredient tea tree oil for the control of botrytis grey mold on greenhouse and outdoor ornamentals, the control of white rust on greenhouse and outdoor chrysanthemums, and the suppression of fusarium wilt on greenhouse and outdoor carnations.
Bruce C Nuclear Project - Public Comments Invited and Information Sessions on the Draft Integrated Tailored Impact Statement Guidelines and Public Participation Plan Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society June 12, 2025 to July 11, 2025 2025-06-12 2025-07-11 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada June 12, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada and the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission invite public comments by July 11, 2025 on the draft Integrated Tailored Impact Statement Guidelines and the draft public participation plan.
Share your input on Measures to Collect Health Information with the Centre for Direct Health Measures Closed Health May 20, 2025 to July 11, 2025 2025-05-20 2025-07-11 Statistics Canada The Canadian Health Measures Survey (CHMS) began collection in 2007 to gather information to improve the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of illnesses and to promote the health and wellness of Canadians. This survey asks questions about health history and health-related lifestyle behaviours along with direct measurements of a range of health factors including physical measures (e.g., height, weight, blood pressure, results from fitness tests) and lab analysis of blood and urine samples. The data collected is used to establish and adjust Canadian benchmarks for a range of health indicators and allows researchers to further understand the relationships between disease risk factors and health conditions such as obesity, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and exposure to infectious diseases and environmental contaminants. The directly measured data developed by the CHMS complements data from self-reported surveys and administrative sources, addressing the gaps in each to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the health of Canadians. To minimize the burden on survey participants, the CHMS seeks to explore new ways to connect with and engage Canadians for direct health measures. This includes the voluntary sharing of health data, such as step count activity levels, self-collected through consumer smart devices. The goal of this engagement activity is to better understand how Canadians prefer to be engaged by Statistics Canada and their views on the use of data from digital technologies and other direct measures. These insights will help Statistics Canada design its products and services to better meet the health data needs of Canadians.
Path forward for evaluating policy options for managing the accumulation of oil sands mine water in tailings ponds Closed Development | Economy | Economic development | Environment | Fisheries | Health | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Policy | Private sector | Public safety | Regulations | Science and technology Mar 7, 2025 to July 7, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-07-07 Environment and Climate Change Canada This discussion paper was prepared collaboratively with CIWG members at the request of ECCC to provide interested parties with an update on the work completed at the CIWG to date, an overview of the path forward, and an opportunity for the public to provide feedback to ECCC. Any interested parties may comment in writing by e-mail at sb-os@ec.gc.ca or mail.
Accesibility Internal Questionnaire Closed Gender Based Analysis | Human resources | Persons with disabilities | Service | Science and technology June 16, 2025 to July 4, 2025 2025-06-16 2025-07-04 Canada Border Services Agency Obtain feedback from employees regarding accessibility at the CBSA What We Heard Report available
Regulations Amending the Pest Control Products Regulations (Antimicrobial-treated Class I Medical Devices), Notice of Intent NOI2025-01 Closed Health | Regulations June 2, 2025 to July 2, 2025 2025-06-02 2025-07-02 Health Canada The purpose of this notice is to inform stakeholders and people in Canada that Health Canada is seeking feedback on a proposed amendment to the Pest Control Products Regulations. The amendment would exempt Class I medical devices (for example, wheelchairs, manual toothbrushes, compression stockings) treated with antimicrobial preservatives as well as the corresponding antimicrobials when used to treat those devices, from the Pest Control Products Act, as it has been determined that the risks of these products are adequately addressed under the Food and Drugs Act and the Medical Devices Regulations.
Engagement on Intentions Paper for a Carbon Capture, Utilization and Storage Framework in Canada's offshore areas. Closed Environment | Natural resources Mar 7, 2025 to June 30, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-06-30 Natural Resources Canada Fuels Sector intends to publish an intentions paper on the Let’s Talk Natural Resources website to obtain initial views from the public on the proposed approach and elements of the legislative framework for carbon capture, storage and utilization (CCUS) in offshore areas. There would be a 30-day comment period. The feedback will help to shape options for a Memorandum to Cabinet and subsequent engagement requirements during legislative development.
Invitation to participate in the revision of the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Canada Open - accepting input Industry Aug 24, 2023 to June 30, 2025 2023-08-24 2025-06-30 Statistics Canada Statistics Canada invites data producers and data users, representatives of business associations, government bodies at the federal, provincial and local levels, academics and researchers and all other interested parties to submit proposals for the revision to the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Canada.We are seeking proposals for changes for two main reasons: collect input from data producers and users as an integral part of the NAICS revision process, and ensure users' needs continue to be met, therefore the classification remains relevant. What We Heard Report available
Invitation to participate in the revision of the North American Product Classification System (NAPCS) Canada Open - accepting input Industry Aug 25, 2023 to June 30, 2025 2023-08-25 2025-06-30 Statistics Canada Statistics Canada invites data producers and data users, representatives of business associations, government bodies at the federal, provincial and local levels, academics and researchers and all other interested parties to submit proposals for the revision to the North American Product Classification System (NAPCS) Canada.We are seeking proposals for changes for two main reasons: collect input from data producers and users as an integral part of the NAPCS Canada revision process, and ensure users' needs continue to be met, therefore the classification remains relevant. What We Heard Report available
Let’s Talk Transportation: Environmental Effects Evaluation of the Lac-Mégantic Railway Bypass Project Closed Transportation May 16, 2025 to June 30, 2025 2025-05-16 2025-06-30 Transport Canada This consultation will provide an opportunity to obtain feedback on the Environmental Effects Evaluation report before the railway operator submits it as part of the application to the Canadian Transportation Agency. This Environmental Effects Evaluation report combines various studies, some of which have previously been consulted with citizens. This updated version, produced by the rail operator, lists and describes all the potential environmental impacts of the Lac-Mégantic rail bypass.
Leased Aircraft Standard Amendment Package Closed Transportation May 27, 2025 to June 26, 2025 2025-05-27 2025-06-26 Transport Canada In response to the recent ICAO audit, Transport Canada proposes to amend Standard 223 to better align with ICAO norms. Specifically, the proposed amendments contained in the present NPA serve to: • clarify requirements for the acceptance or approval of aircraft leasing arrangements; • establish processes for the acceptance or approval of aircraft leasing arrangements, including those linked to airworthiness; • authorize foreign operators’ maintenance release procedures, linked to the requirement that an air operator can only operate a leased aircraft if it has been maintained in accordance with procedures authorized by the state of registry; • establish a new requirement for foreign civil aviation authority to authorize Canadian operators’ maintenance procedures for aircraft in that foreign registry; • clarify procedures for determining the responsibility for maintenance release and for the airworthiness of aircraft; • establish procedures to consider and record the responsibility for signing maintenance release; and • ensure compliance with airworthiness directives.
Accessibility Standards Canada: 2026 to 2028 Accessibility Plan Closed Human resources | Persons with disabilities | Policy | Service June 9, 2025 to June 23, 2025 2025-06-09 2025-06-23 Accessibility Standards Canada We held a consultation on our Accessibility Plan in line with the Accessible Canada Act and Accessible Canada Regulations. The objective was to inform the plan through consulting with people with disabilities. We understand the problems that arise from consulting the same groups too often. We chose to consult specific people who often interact with us. These groups included: (1) our employees; (2) members of our Board of Directors; (3) members of our technical committees who are responsible for drafting accessibility standards; and (4) recipients of research funding from our Grants and Contributions Program. We sent an email invitation to complete our online survey to a total of 286 people. Alternative ways to complete the survey were offered. Results are being analyzed and will inform our next Accessibility Plan. What We Heard Report available
Engagement process on the development of a strategic environmental assessment for the Town of Banff Community Plan Closed Communication | Economic development May 22, 2025 to June 23, 2025 2025-05-22 2025-06-23 Parks Canada Parks Canada is undertaking an engagement process on the development of a strategic environmental assessment for the Town of Banff Community Plan. Community plans are long range documents that outline a vision for what the community should look like in the future, followed by direction on how to achieve that vision. The Town of Banff is currently updating their community plan, with the second phase of the plan engagement underway. To get a copy and provide feedback on the draft Town of Banff Community Plan, please visit: https://banff.ca/251/Banff-Community-Plan. To assess the positive and negative environmental impacts and understand the potential for cumulative effects, a strategic environmental assessment on the Town of Banff Community Plan is required. The strategic environmental assessment (SEA) will provide the opportunity to incorporate environmental considerations, particularly cumulative effects considerations, while the plan is still under development. In the first step of this engagement process, Parks Canada is seeking feedback from all Canadians on the draft Terms of Reference for the SEA. The Terms of Reference will provide the parameters of what the Town of Banff will need to assess in the SEA. Feedback from this phase will be used to inform any necessary changes to the terms of reference.
Consultation on metaldehyde, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-06 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Mar 27, 2025 to June 10, 2025 2025-03-27 2025-06-10 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for metaldehyde.
York Factory First Nation Ten Shilling Aerodrome Project Public notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society May 7, 2025 to June 6, 2025 2025-05-07 2025-06-06 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada May 7, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for the proposed York Factory First Nation Ten Shilling Aerodrome Project, a new aerodrome along Ten Shilling Creek, approximately four kilometres south of York Factory settlement and National History Site, in northeastern Manitoba.
Consultation: Towards a pathway for health products containing cannabidiol Closed Food and drug | Health Mar 7, 2025 to June 5, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-06-05 Health Canada As indicated in the 2024-2026 Forward Regulatory Plan, Health Canada is exploring a regulatory pathway for the authorization of health products containing cannabidiol, commonly referred to as CBD, that can be accessed without a prescription. Our goal is to protect the health and safety of Canadians and enable access to safe, effective, and high-quality products that are supported by adequate evidence for human and animal use to treat specific or minor ailments.
Consultation on fatty acid cluster and its associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-05 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Mar 7, 2025 to June 5, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-06-05 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of products containing fatty acids, potassium salts of fatty acids, triethanolamine salts of fatty acids and ammonium salt of fatty acid and associated end-use products registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed updates to standard label statements.
Engagement with new stakeholders on matters of public policy relating to women and gender equality and youth Closed Economy | Employment | Gender | Housing | Indigenous Peoples | Policy | Youth Jan 15, 2024 to June 2, 2025 2024-01-15 2025-06-02 Women and Gender Equality Canada The goal of this engagement is to facilitate discussion with WAGE stakeholders at the local and community level with whom the Minister has not previously engaged, on matters of public policy related to women and gender equality and youth. These engagements will help ensure that lesser heard voices are represented in government policy priorities.
Consultation on Cellulose (from powdered corn cobs) and its associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-04 Closed Health Mar 3, 2025 to June 1, 2025 2025-03-03 2025-06-01 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of cellulose (from powdered corn cobs) and associated end-use products registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed updates to risk mitigation measures on the label.
Watchlist 2025 mid-cycle consultations with industry Open - accepting input Transportation Oct 7, 2024 to May 31, 2025 2024-10-07 2025-05-31 Transportation Safety Board of Canada The TSB is launching it's mid-cycle consultations for the 2025 Watchlist. This program highlights key safety deficiencies in the air, marine and rail industries. Mid-cycle consultations encompasses a questionnaire to stakeholders, and attend industry event for the air and marine modes. The results will help inform Watchlist 2025.

Consultation on the proposed Release Guidelines for Chemicals Used in the Rubber Products Manufacturing Sector (BENPAT and TMTD) Closed Environment Mar 22, 2025 to May 21, 2025 2025-03-22 2025-05-21 Environment and Climate Change Canada The intent of the consultation is to provide stakeholders on the consultation on the proposed guidelines (including BENPAT and TMTD).
Proposal to modify the use of caffeine as a supplemental ingredient Closed Food and drug | Health Mar 7, 2025 to May 21, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-05-21 Health Canada In light of the Food and Nutrition Directorate's conclusion that information related to the safety of caffeine supports its use as a supplemental ingredient in the foods of interest, Health Canada proposes to enable the use of caffeine as a supplemental ingredient in foods listed in item 6 of the List of Permitted Supplemented Food Categories, and to update the cautionary statements for the already permitted solid supplemented food categories (that is, chocolate confectioneries, protein-isolate- and cereal-based bars, and chewing gums) with high caffeine content by modifying the List of Permitted Supplemental Ingredients
Consultation on draft guidance on co-packaged drug products Closed Food and drug | Health Mar 18, 2025 to May 18, 2025 2025-03-18 2025-05-18 Health Canada Health Canada has drafted a guidance document on co-packaged drug products. This document explains the regulatory requirements for co-packaged drug products and their components. This draft guidance is open for consultation.
Baffinland Steensby Railway Consultation Closed Transportation Mar 28, 2025 to May 16, 2025 2025-03-28 2025-05-16 Canadian Transportation Agency The Canadian Transportation Agency (Agency) is providing an opportunity for the public to comment on the application by Baffinland Iron Mines Corporation (Baffinland) to construct an approximately 149 km railway line (Steensby Railway) from the Mary River Mine site to the port located in Steensby Inlet (Steensby Port).

Adding Capacity to Sainte-Marguerite-3 Generating Station Project Public Notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Apr 9, 2025 to May 9, 2025 2025-04-09 2025-05-09 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada April 9, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for the proposed Adding Capacity to Sainte-Marguerite-3 Generating Station Project, which involves the installation of a third generating unit, as well as the construction of an underground penstock at the existing Sainte-Marguerite-3 power station located near Sept-Îles, Quebec.
Updated Draft Assessment of Boric Acid, its salts and its precursors and the Revised Risk Management Scope. Closed Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Mar 8, 2025 to May 7, 2025 2025-03-08 2025-05-07 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Updated Draft Assessment of Boric Acid, its salts and its precursors and the Revised Risk Management Scope published in the Canada Gazette, Part I, Volume 159, Number 10 on March 8, 2025
Risk Management Approach for the Benzotriazoles and Benzothiazoles Group Closed Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Mar 8, 2025 to May 7, 2025 2025-03-08 2025-05-07 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Risk Management Approach for the Benzotriazoles and Benzothiazoles Group published in the Canada Gazette, Part 1, Volume 159 Number 10 on on March 8, 2025
Risk Management Approach for PFAS, excluding fluoropolymers Closed Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Mar 8, 2025 to May 7, 2025 2025-03-08 2025-05-07 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Risk Management Approach for PFAS, excluding fluoropolymers published in the Canada Gazette, Part 1, Volume 159 Number 10 on March 8, 2025
Floating Platform for Boat Davits at the Canadian Coast Guard College Closed Environment | Fisheries | Regulations Apr 7, 2025 to May 7, 2025 2025-04-07 2025-05-07 Fisheries and Oceans Canada Fisheries and Oceans Canada (The Canadian Coast Guard College) and Transport Canada are considering comments received to help inform their determination on whether the carrying out of the project is likely to cause significant adverse environmental effects.
Consultation on proposed amendments to the Pest Control Products Regulations (Product Exemptions) - Pre-consultation, PRO2025-01 Closed Agriculture | Health Mar 7, 2025 to May 6, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-05-06 Health Canada The Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on Regulatory Proposal PRO2025-01: Pre-Consultation – Proposed Amendments to the Pest Control Products Regulations (Product Exemptions). The PMRA will accept written comments from the public on this proposal for up to 60 days from the date of publication of this document.
Consultation: Draft Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, Arsenic Closed Food and drug | Health | Public safety Mar 7, 2025 to May 6, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-05-06 Health Canada Health Canada has drafted guidelines for drinking water quality related to arsenic. We’re looking for feedback, in particular on the approach used to develop the guidelines and on the potential costs of implementing them.
Consultation on Proposed Amendments to the Pest Control Products Regulations (Pest Control Product Devices) - Pre-consultation, PRO2025-02 Closed Agriculture | Health Mar 7, 2025 to May 6, 2025 2025-03-07 2025-05-06 Health Canada The Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on Regulatory Proposal PRO2025-02, Pre-Consultation – Proposed Amendments to the Pest Control Products Regulations (Pest Control Devices). The PMRA will accept written comments from the public on this proposal for up to 60 days from the date of publication of this document.
Share your thoughts on Canadian content for audio services Closed Arts | Communication | Culture | Policy Feb 20, 2025 to May 5, 2025 2025-02-20 2025-05-05 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission We are seeking your comments on the creation, distribution and discoverability of Canadian and Indigenous audio content across radio and online streaming platforms. This includes examining how traditional broadcasters and audio online streaming services can best contribute to a sustainable broadcasting system that ensures that Canadians have access to the audio content and music they want. The CRTC will update the definition of Canadian content for audio services to help ensure that content such as music is made, shared, and discovered on all platforms.
Proposal to transfer the maximum levels for ethyl carbamate in alcoholic beverages to the List of Contaminants and Other Adulterating Substances in Foods Closed Food and drug | Health Feb 19, 2025 to May 5, 2025 2025-02-19 2025-05-05 Health Canada Health Canada is proposing to transfer the MLs for ethyl carbamate in alcoholic beverages from the List of Maximum Levels for Various Chemical Contaminants in Foods, to the regulatory List of Contaminants and Other Adulterating Substances in Foods, maintaining the current value of the MLs but amending some of the product categories to better reflect the diverse market of alcoholic beverages.
Draft Assessment of 14 substances in the Tricylcic Sesquiterpenes and Triterpeniods Group and the Risk Management Scope Closed Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Mar 1, 2025 to Apr 30, 2025 2025-03-01 2025-04-30 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Draft assessment of 14 substances in the Tricylcic Sesquiterpenes and Triterpeniods Group and the Risk Management Scope published in the Canada Gazette, Part 1, Volume 159, Number 9 on March 1, 2025
Consultation on Streptomyces lydicus Strain WYEC 108 and its associated end-use products, Proposed Re-evaluation Decision PRVD2025-01 Closed Food and drug | Health Jan 30, 2025 to Apr 30, 2025 2025-01-30 2025-04-30 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting Canadians on the proposal to continue registration of Streptomyces lydicus strain WYEC 108 and associated end-use products registered for sale and use in Canada, with proposed updates to standard label statements.
Cedar LNG Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited on Proposed Amendments to the Decision Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Mar 31, 2025 to Apr 30, 2025 2025-03-31 2025-04-30 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada March 31, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period on proposed amendments to the decision statement for the Cedar LNG Project, a floating liquified natural gas (LNG) processing facility and marine export terminal located near Kitimat, British Columbia.
Collaborative Geospatial Data Strategy for Canada Closed Natural resources | Science and technology Apr 10, 2025 to Apr 30, 2025 2025-04-10 2025-04-30 Natural Resources Canada Using NRCan's online EngagementHQ platform and through a series of thematic virtual round table discussions, engage with producers and users of geospatial data in industry, academia, Indigenous organizations, NGOs, and governments on future needs and opportunities to modernize and strengthen Canada's Geospatial Data Infrastructure, including governance and institutions, legal and policy framework, financial resources, innovation, data, standards, partnerships, capacity and education, and communications and engagement.
Notice of Consultation on the Prescription Drug List (PDL): Nicotine Tablets Closed Food and drug | Health Feb 13, 2025 to Apr 29, 2025 2025-02-13 2025-04-29 Health Canada The purpose of this Notice of Consultation is to provide an opportunity for the public and other interested stakeholders to comment on the proposal to revise the listing for "Nicotine or its salts" on the human use part of the Prescription Drug List (PDL). The proposal is to allow the non-prescription use of orally disintegrating or sublingual tablets by: adding a new part "g" to the qualifier of the listing to specifically exempt orally disintegrating or sublingual tablets containing 4 milligrams or less of nicotine per dosage unit.
Notice of Consultation on the Prescription Drug List (PDL): Nicotine Buccal Pouches Closed Food and drug | Health Feb 13, 2025 to Apr 29, 2025 2025-02-13 2025-04-29 Health Canada The purpose of this Notice of Consultation is to provide an opportunity for the public and other interested stakeholders to comment on the proposal to revise the listing for "Nicotine or its salts" on the human use part of the Prescription Drug List (PDL). The proposal is to clarify the non-prescription use of nicotine buccal pouches by: amending part "d" of the qualifier of the listing to be specific to sprays and inhalers only, and adding a new part "f" to the qualifier that is specific to nicotine buccal pouches.
Adding Capacity to Sainte-Marguerite-3 Generating Station Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited on the Summary of the Initial Project Description Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Apr 7, 2025 to Apr 26, 2025 2025-04-07 2025-04-26 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada April 7, 2025 — Hydro-Québec is proposing to increase the capacity of the Sainte-Marguerite-3 hydroelectric power station located near Sept-Îles, Quebec. The project would involve installing a third generating unit, as well as constructing an underground penstock at the existing power station. The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) invites Indigenous Peoples and the public to review the summary of the Initial Project Description and provide comments on the proposed project. This feedback will help IAAC prepare a summary of issues that will be given to the proponent. Following the proponent's response to this summary of issues, IAAC will determine whether an impact assessment is required.
Consultation: Health Canada and the Public Health Agency of Canada Accessibility Plans Closed Health | Persons with disabilities Mar 3, 2025 to Apr 25, 2025 2025-03-03 2025-04-25 Health Canada The Accessibility Readiness Team (ART) at Health Canada (HC) and the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) invites you to provide feedback on accessibility at HC and PHAC. In accordance with the Accessible Canada Act, we at ART are currently developing the 2026-2028 Accessibility Plans for HC and for PHAC. We are seeking feedback from Canadians on any barriers to accessibility that persons with disabilities or their caregivers may have encountered when interacting with HC or PHAC, at any time. Your feedback is important and will help us achieve a barrier-free Canada by 2040.
Lynn Lake Gold Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited on Proposed Amendments to the Decision Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Mar 25, 2025 to Apr 24, 2025 2025-03-25 2025-04-24 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada March 25, 2025— The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period as part of the post-decision phase for the Lynn Lake Gold Project, an open-pit gold mine located north of Winnipeg, near Lynn Lake, Manitoba.
James Bay Lithium Mine Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited on Proposed Amendments to the Decision Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Mar 25, 2025 to Apr 24, 2025 2025-03-25 2025-04-24 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada March 25, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period as part of the post-decision phase for the James Bay Lithium Mine Project, an open-pit lithium mine located east of James Bay and the Eastmain Cree Community, in Quebec.
Standard 425 - Flight Training Closed Transportation Mar 19, 2025 to Apr 23, 2025 2025-03-19 2025-04-23 Transport Canada As part of a pilot project with a limited number of flight schools, Transport Canada (TC) has been assessing the suitability of electrically powered aircraft in a flight training environment. While results are so far promising, preliminary evaluations highlighted the need to establish a regulatory framework to mitigate potential risks associated with limited battery endurance and other concerns associated with the operation of this novel powerplant. The objective of the proposed revisions to the standards is to establish that framework by requiring flight schools that are using electrically powered aircraft to develop a TC-approved training program in order to identify the potential risks and describe the mitigation measures they will implement.
Final Capital Adequacy Requirements (CAR) Guideline (2026) Closed Finance Feb 20, 2025 to Apr 22, 2025 2025-02-20 2025-04-22 Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Canada The Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI) published the final Capital Adequacy Requirements (CAR) Guideline (2026) on November 20, 2025 following a 60-day public consultation on the CAR Guideline with related revisions to the Small and Medium-Sized Deposit-Taking Institutions (SMSB) Capital and Liquidity Requirements Guideline. What We Heard Report available
Consultations on Potential Trade Measures to Prevent Diversion of Steel Products into Canada Closed Economy Mar 22, 2025 to Apr 21, 2025 2025-03-22 2025-04-21 Department of Finance Canada The global steel market has a severe overcapacity which results in an excess of artificially low-priced foreign steel and contributes to depressed prices in Canada and globally, threatening Canadian steel workers and businesses. Following recent U.S. tariffs, there is a heightened risk that these steel products may flood the Canadian market putting both producers and workers at risk. Recognizing the harm that increased imports into Canada may cause to Canadian steel producers and workers, the Government of Canada consulted on possible trade measures to protect against the potential diversion of steel products into the Canadian market. This engagement process gave all interested members of the public the opportunity to provide input on this topic.
Consultation: Proposal to amend the list of maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs in foods - Proposed MRL 2025-1 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Feb 5, 2025 to Apr 21, 2025 2025-02-05 2025-04-21 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking comments on a proposal to amend the List of Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for Veterinary Drugs in Foods (the List).
Share your views: Consultation on the draft guidance on managing applications for medical device licences Closed Health Feb 20, 2025 to Apr 21, 2025 2025-02-20 2025-04-21 Health Canada Health Canada has updated the "Guidance on the Management of Applications for Medical Device Licences". This document explains to medical device manufacturers how medical device licence applications submitted under the "Medical Devices Regulations" are managed. Health Canada invites you to provide feedback on the draft guidance document.
Consultation on lambda-cyhalothrin, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-03 Closed Food and drug | Health Feb 3, 2025 to Apr 19, 2025 2025-02-03 2025-04-19 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for lambda-cyhalothrin up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 19 April 2025).
TP 7775 – Procedures for the Certification of Aerodromes as Airports, 3rd edition Closed Transportation Mar 20, 2025 to Apr 19, 2025 2025-03-20 2025-04-19 Transport Canada Transport Canada Civil Aviation (TCCA)’s objective is a full review and rewrite of 7775 to increase its functionality as a certification procedure document and to bring its content into alignment with the applicable elements of Canada’s regulatory framework.
Consultation on pelargonic acid and Beloukha Herbicide, Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-02 Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health | Plants Mar 3, 2025 to Apr 17, 2025 2025-03-03 2025-04-17 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Pelargonic Acid Technical and Beloukha Herbicide, belonging to Belchim Crop Protection Canada Inc., containing the active ingredient pelargonic acid, to control or suppress weeds in greenhouse-grown cucumber, tomato, pepper and lettuce, in greenhouse-grown ornamentals (bedding plants, flowering and non-flowering plants) and around outdoor woody and herbaceous ornamentals, including container-, field-, and liner-grown plants, plants in landscape plantings, landscape trees, nursery trees, shrubs, Christmas trees, and forest and commercial trees.
Call for Expert Supports Relevant to the Regional Assessment in the Ring of Fire Area Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Mar 24, 2025 to Apr 17, 2025 2025-03-24 2025-04-17 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada March 24, 2025 — The Regional Assessment Working Group invites expressions of interest from individuals and organizations that hold information and datasets that would support the regional assessment. Applications received by April 17, 2025, will be considered.
Consultation Document on the Risk Management of Short-chain, Medium-chain and Long-chain chlorinated alkanes Closed Environment | Health | Regulations Feb 13, 2025 to Apr 14, 2025 2025-02-13 2025-04-14 Environment and Climate Change Canada The purpose of this consultation is to inform and solicit comments from stakeholders on risk management of chlorinated alkanes.
Consultation on tebuconazole, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-02 Closed Food and drug | Health Jan 28, 2025 to Apr 13, 2025 2025-01-28 2025-04-13 Health Canada Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA) invites the public to submit written comments on the proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) for tebuconazole up to 75 days from the date of publication of this consultation document (by 13 April 2025).
2024 Interswitching consultation Closed Transportation Nov 21, 2024 to Apr 11, 2025 2024-11-21 2025-04-11 Canadian Transportation Agency The CTA invites stakeholders to share their views to ensure the Railway Interswitching Regulations are clear, and the rates methodology is fair and reasonable to all parties.
Management Plan for the Anticosti Aster (Symphyotrichum anticostense) in Canada [Proposed] Closed Animals | Environment Feb 6, 2025 to Apr 7, 2025 2025-02-06 2025-04-07 Environment and Climate Change Canada Formal consultation with Indigenous partners on the proposed posting of Anticosti Aster Management Plan.
Share your thoughts: Participate in the risk assessment process for GloFish® Corydoras Closed Environment | Health | Science and technology Mar 8, 2025 to Apr 6, 2025 2025-03-08 2025-04-06 Environment and Climate Change Canada The federal government, through Environment and Climate Change Canada and Health Canada, is asking Canadians, industry, non-governmental organizations, and Indigenous Peoples to share their ideas and suggestions, through email, mail, workshops and online engagement. Feedback will help develop a renewed federal chemicals management program to protect human health and the environment.
Consultation: Draft Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, Trihalomethanes Closed Environment | Health Jan 10, 2025 to Apr 4, 2025 2025-01-10 2025-04-04 Health Canada Health Canada is updating its guidance on trihalomethanes (THMs) in drinking water. This guideline technical document outlines the evaluation of the available information on THMs. We invite you to provide comments on the proposed guideline, on the approach used for its development, and on the potential economic costs of implementing it.
Notice of Intent to Impose Countermeasures in Response to United States Tariffs on Canadian Goods Closed Economy Mar 4, 2025 to Apr 2, 2025 2025-03-04 2025-04-02 Department of Finance Canada As announced on March 4, 2025, and in response to unjustified U.S. tariffs, the federal government imposed 25 per cent tariffs on goods worth $30 billion and initiated consultations on an additional list of goods. The government may draw from this list to impose additional tariff countermeasures on imports from the U.S., if the current U.S. tariffs are maintained or new tariffs are announced. The consultation on the additional list of goods gave all interested members of the public the opportunity to provide input.
Proposal to modify the Table of Permitted Nutrient Content Statements and Claims (free of sugars) Closed Food and drug | Health Jan 17, 2025 to Apr 2, 2025 2025-01-17 2025-04-02 Health Canada The updated regulations included modifications to the energy requirements of the "free of sugars" claim (item 37 of the table following section B.01.513). These changes, which were intended to make the energy requirements less restrictive, unintentionally affected chewing gums. As a result, chewing gums that were previously exempt from these requirements may no longer be in compliance with the regulations after the transition period ends on December 31, 2025.
Onshore Pipeline Regulations and Filing Manuals Review Project Closed Environment | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Regulations Feb 1, 2021 to Mar 31, 2025 2021-02-01 2025-03-31 Canada Energy Regulator The CER is conducting a comprehensive review of the OPR under the CER Act to update the regulations. The CER’s objective for the OPR Review is to deliver a regulation that supports the highest level of safety, security and environmental protection, advances Reconciliation with Indigenous peoples, addresses transparency and inclusive participation, provides for predictable and timely oversight and encourages innovation. The CER conducted the first phase of engagement in 2022, with a Discussion Paper and engagement sessions. The CER is also updating various sections of its Filing Manuals to reflect changing requirements, conditions, regulations, industry practices, and feedback received from regulated companies, Indigenous Peoples and other interested persons or groups. The CER will be coordinating engagement activities for the Review of the OPR and Filing Manuals, to share learnings and consolidate input across common topics areas. When available, details on how and when to participate in the next phase of engagement will be posted on the CER Dialogue website.
National Restorative Justice Research Network Closed Justice and the Law Jan 1, 2024 to Mar 31, 2025 2024-01-01 2025-03-31 Department of Justice Canada Justice Canada committed to developing a National Restorative Justice Research Network (Research Network) recognizing the important role that data, research and evaluation play in helping to increase the use of restorative justice. The Research Network will support collaboration between Indigenous and non-Indigenous government, non-government, community, and academic researchers to help raise awareness about restorative justice and support policy and program development to increase its use in Canada. A multi-phased engagement process will be undertaken to collaboratively co-design the Research Network.
Notice of intent to make a Ministerial Exemption Order to permit continued supply of naloxone kits on the Canadian market Closed Food and drug | Health | Regulations Mar 1, 2025 to Mar 31, 2025 2025-03-01 2025-03-31 Health Canada The proposed EO would address feedback received on the interim policy and allow for the transition to a formal regulatory instrument. Health Canada’s objective is to help ensure continued access to naloxone kits on the Canadian market, while maintaining the quality, safety, efficacy and traceability of these kits.
Government of Canada Artificial Intelligence Register Open - accepting input Development | Industry | Policy | Service | Science and technology Jan 19, 2025 to Mar 31, 2025 2025-01-19 2025-03-31 Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat This consultation is a chance for people in Canada to give their views on the Government of Canada’s first AI Register, which was published in November 2025. This version of the GC AI Register is a minimum viable product (MVP), or an early version with only basic features and content. It was released as an MVP so that we could use it to engage the public on the content and functions they would like to see in future versions. This feedback will be used to develop an improved version of the GC AI Register that will replace the MVP and be updated regularly.
Consultation on benzovindiflupyr, Proposed Maximum Residue Limit PMRL2025-01 Closed Food and drug | Health Jan 14, 2025 to Mar 30, 2025 2025-01-14 2025-03-30 Health Canada Health Canada is consulting the public and seeking your feedback on a proposed maximum residue limit (MRL) increase for the post emergent foliar use of benzovindiflupyr on lowbush blueberries.
Options to expand Transportation Security Clearance requirements Closed Transportation Jan 30, 2025 to Mar 30, 2025 2025-01-30 2025-03-30 Transport Canada A Let's Talk Transportation online consultation was held for 60 days to solicit stakeholder feedback on options to expand Transportation Security Clearance requirements, to traditional occupations and ports across Canada. During the 60-day timeframe, in person and virtual engagement sessions were also held with key stakeholders including port authorities labour groups, and employer representatives.
Proposed Registration Decision PRD2025-01, Flg22-Bt Peptide, Vismax Seed Treatment, and Vismax Row Crop Closed Agriculture | Food and drug | Health Feb 12, 2025 to Mar 29, 2025 2025-02-12 2025-03-29 Health Canada Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA), pursuant to subsection 28(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, is proposing registration for the sale and use of Flg22-Bt Peptide Technical, Vismax Seed Treatment, and Vismax Row Crop, containing the active ingredient Flg22-Bt peptide, to control fungal plant disease caused by Fusarium species in soybean and wheat.
Regional Assessment of the St. Lawrence River Area Public Notice Public Comment Period on the draft Terms of Reference Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Feb 27, 2025 to Mar 29, 2025 2025-02-27 2025-03-29 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada Ottawa – February 27, 2025 – The Impact Assessment Agency of Canda (IAAC), in partnership with the Mohawk Council of Kahnawà:ke (MCK) and other interested First Nations, is conducting the Regional Assessment of the St. Lawrence River Area (SLRA). Regional assessments aim to better understand the potential effects of past, present and future development. They can be used to inform, among other things, future project-specific federal impact assessments and decisions in this area.
Draft Assessment for the Fourteen Terpene and Terpenoid Substances Group and the Risk Management Scope Closed Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Jan 25, 2025 to Mar 26, 2025 2025-01-25 2025-03-26 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Draft assessment for the Fourteen Terpene and Terpenoid Substances Group and the Risk Management Scope published in the Canada Gazette, Part I, Volume 159, Number 4 on January 25, 2025
The Additional Risk Characterization Document for human health and the Risk Management Scope for melamine Closed Environment | Exporting/Importing | Food and drug | Health | Industry Jan 25, 2025 to Mar 26, 2025 2025-01-25 2025-03-26 Environment and Climate Change Canada To seek comments on the Additional Risk Characterization Document for human health and the Risk Management Scope for melamine published in the Canada Gazette, Part I, Volume 159, Number 4 on January 25, 2025
Seeking feedback on regulatory amendments to the Immigration and Refugee Protection Regulations (IRPR) Closed Economy | Immigration | Regulations Feb 21, 2025 to Mar 24, 2025 2025-02-21 2025-03-24 Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Immigration is a shared federal and provincial/territorial responsibility. Duplication has been identified in the administration of the Provincial Nominee Program (PNP), particularly in the assessment and processing of PNP applications. Consequently, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) sought feedback from the public on regulatory amendments to make provinces and territories solely responsible for eligibility assessment under the PNP, and thus create efficiencies in processing.
Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project Public Notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Feb 21, 2025 to Mar 24, 2025 2025-02-21 2025-03-24 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada February 21, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment process for the proposed Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project, a new open-pit and underground lithium mine located east of Radisson, Quebec.
Share your input on Statistics Canada’s service to the accessibility and disability community Closed Persons with disabilities | Science and technology Dec 4, 2024 to Mar 21, 2025 2024-12-04 2025-03-21 Statistics Canada In 2019, the Accessible Canada Act was published. The Act commits the government and all other federally regulated entities to become fully accessible by 2040. Government departments must identify, remove, and prevent barriers to accessibility. statistics Canada is committed to ensuring our products and services are accessible by design and allows all Canadians to access them. The purpose of this engagement activity is to understand the perspective of persons with lived experience with disability, external partners, and stakeholders. These perspectives will help Statistics Canada better design its products and services to serve the accessibility and disability community in Canada. The goal is to help identify, prevent, and eliminate any barriers persons with disabilities may experience to access and use Statistics Canada’s services and products. The input collected will be used to: draft Statistics Canada’s 2026-2029 Accessibility Plan; support modifications to hiring policies and protocols; inform how various accessibility pillars address barriers to accessibility in their areas of responsibility; inform how Statistics Canada can adapt data releases to be more inclusive and barrier-free; and, further learn about how to make surveys and survey data accessible and approachable. This will help ensure data is not only accessible but also more representative of and relevant to all Canadians. What We Heard Report available
Share your input on Statistics Canada’s Direct Health Measures Programs Closed Health Jan 9, 2025 to Mar 21, 2025 2025-01-09 2025-03-21 Statistics Canada The Centre for Direct Health Measures (CDHM) is responsible for the production of statistics on health indicators that require direct measurements of physical health at the population level across several areas, such as chronic conditions, oral health, infectious disease, and environmental contaminants. As the CDHM envisions the future of their programs, they will, for instance, review and update survey designs, collection infrastructures, survey content, and analytical plans. Statistics Canada (StatCan) is committed to the provision of quality data. As part of the Direct Health Measures Programs, the CDHM seeks feedback from key stakeholders, such as government health entities, pan-Canadian organizations, provincial and territorial Statistical Focal Points, academic researchers, and non-government organizations. These perspectives will help StatCan’s CDHM to plan the development of existing programs such as the Canadian Health Measures Survey (CHMS), as well as to design and implement new programs to meet stakeholder needs. The goal is to identify data needs and gaps, raise awareness of StatCan data holdings, learn from stakeholder subject matter expertise and explore potential collaboration. What We Heard Report available
Share you feedback on the Special Edition issue of Insights on Canadian Society Closed Communication Feb 13, 2025 to Mar 21, 2025 2025-02-13 2025-03-21 Statistics Canada Insights on Canadian Society (ICS) is Statistics Canada’s flagship social publication, which brings together a wide range of data sources to provide information on various aspects of Canadian society, including labour, income, education, social, and demographic issues that affect the lives of Canadians. In the interest of modernising the dissemination of social insights, Statistics Canada is expanding ICS to include a Special Edition, which will offer diverse products, like shorter analytical articles, so that readers can interactively examine different facets of our stories under a broad theme. As part of this initiative, virtual group discussions will be held to seek feedback on the Special Edition’s utility and accessibility, to better understand its relevancy and value to readers.
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH Guideline E6(R3) Annex 2: Good Clinical Practice Closed Food and drug | Health Dec 20, 2024 to Mar 20, 2025 2024-12-20 2025-03-20 Health Canada Please note that draft (step 2) guidelines are only available in English; however, guidelines finalised by the ICH will be translated into French and will be made available upon request. It is also important to note that amendments to draft documents may occur as a result of regulatory consultations and subsequent deliberations within the ICH. All comments forwarded to Health Canada will be transmitted to the ICH as is, with the disclaimer that they are provided for information and do not necessarily represent the views of Health Canada, except as specifically indicated in separate comments. As appropriate, your organization may alternatively wish to provide comments to your affiliate association internationally for their input directly to ICH. Please use the ICH template for public consultations to send in your comments. Comments provided to Health Canada should be submitted by the date listed in the table above in order to allow sufficient time for their assessment and subsequent transmission to the ICH. Comments should be directed to: Health Canada - ICH Coordinator E-mail: ich@hc-sc.gc.ca
Release of draft (Step 2) ICH Guideline M15: Model Informed Drug Development Closed Food and drug | Health Dec 20, 2024 to Mar 20, 2025 2024-12-20 2025-03-20 Health Canada Please note that draft (step 2) guidelines are only available in English; however, guidelines finalised by the ICH will be translated into French and will be made available upon request. It is also important to note that amendments to draft documents may occur as a result of regulatory consultations and subsequent deliberations within the ICH. All comments forwarded to Health Canada will be transmitted to the ICH as is, with the disclaimer that they are provided for information and do not necessarily represent the views of Health Canada, except as specifically indicated in separate comments. As appropriate, your organization may alternatively wish to provide comments to your affiliate association internationally for their input directly to ICH. Please use the ICH template for public consultations to send in your comments. Comments provided to Health Canada should be submitted by the date listed in the table above in order to allow sufficient time for their assessment and subsequent transmission to the ICH. Comments should be directed to: Health Canada - ICH Coordinator E-mail: ich@hc-sc.gc.ca
Consultation – Proposed national park in the Teetł’it Gwinjik (Peel River) Watershed Closed Animals | Culture | Employment | Environment | Heritage | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Plants | Recreation Feb 3, 2025 to Mar 17, 2025 2025-02-03 2025-03-17 Parks Canada We want to hear your views on a proposed new national park in the Teetł’it Gwinjik (Peel River) Watershed, Yukon! The Gwich’in Tribal Council, the First Nation of Nacho Nyak Dun, Parks Canada and the Government of Yukon are exploring the potential for a new national park in the Teetł’it Gwinjik (Peel River) Watershed. The Teetł’it Gwinjik (Peel River) Watershed is in northeast Yukon, close to the Yukon-Northwest Territories border. It is a unique northern area of great importance to the Gwich’in and Nacho Nyak Dun, featuring stunning landscapes of forests, free-flowing rivers and wildlife. The feedback received through this online comment card will be part of a feasibility assessment for the proposed national park. A feasibility assessment is a process to help the parties decide whether a national park is both practical and desirable in this area. The answers you provide will help guide next steps and the future of this area.
Public Consultations: Call for submissions regarding Canada’s Annual Report on Human Rights and Free Trade Between Canada and the Republic of Colombia Closed Trade Feb 17, 2025 to Mar 16, 2025 2025-02-17 2025-03-16 Global Affairs Canada Public consultation for input towards the 14th annual report on human rights and free trade in Colombia pursuant to the Agreement concerning Annual Reports on Human Rights and Free Trade Between Canada and the Republic of Colombia
Consulting Canadians on the potential accession of Costa Rica to the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) Closed Agriculture | Environment | Exporting/Importing | Foreign affairs | Gender | Government procurement | Industry | Indigenous Peoples | International | Policy | Private sector | Regulations | Trade Feb 15, 2025 to Mar 16, 2025 2025-02-15 2025-03-16 Global Affairs Canada The Government of Canada is soliciting the views of Canadians on the potential accession of Costa Rica to the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP). What We Heard Report available
Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project Public Notice Comments Invited on the Summary of the Initial Project Description Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Feb 19, 2025 to Mar 16, 2025 2025-02-19 2025-03-16 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada February 19, 2025 — Patriot Battery Metals is proposing the Shaakichiuwaanaan Mining Project, a new open-pit and underground lithium mine located east of Radisson, Quebec. The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) invites the public and Indigenous Peoples to review the summary of the Initial Project Description and provide comments on the proposed project. This feedback will help IAAC prepare a summary of issues that will be given to the proponent.
Share your thoughts: Proposed new livestock feed ingredient –Propionibacterium freudenreichii culture dehydrated Closed Agriculture | Amendments | Animals | Industry | Policy | Regulations Feb 14, 2025 to Mar 14, 2025 2025-02-14 2025-03-14 Canadian Food Inspection Agency The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is seeking comments on the following proposed single ingredient feed (SIF) description for propionibacterium freudenreichii culture dehydrated
Electric Vehicle Supply Chain investment tax credit Closed Taxes Feb 21, 2025 to Mar 14, 2025 2025-02-21 2025-03-14 Department of Finance Canada To further incentivize growth in Canadian electric vehicle (EV) supply chains, Budget 2024 announced a refundable EV Supply Chain investment tax credit equal to 10 per cent of the capital cost of eligible building property used in qualifying EV supply chain segments. The details of the credit were announced in the 2024 Fall Economic Statement. This consultation sought comments and feedback on draft legislative proposals that would allow Canadian taxable corporations that invest in Canada across three EV supply chain segments (EV assembly, EV battery production, and cathode active material production) to receive a refundable investment tax credit on the capital cost of eligible building property used in those EV supply chain segments.
Sorel-Tracy Port Terminal Project Public Notice Participant Funding Available Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Feb 12, 2025 to Mar 14, 2025 2025-02-12 2025-03-14 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada February 12, 2025 — Funding provided by the Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is now available to help Indigenous Peoples and the public participate in the impact assessment for the proposed Sorel-Tracy Port Terminal Project, a new port terminal in the industrial-port zone of Sorel-Tracy, Quebec. Funding is available for eligible individuals and groups to support their participation in the upcoming steps of the impact assessment process. These steps include reviewing and providing comments on the proponent's Impact Statement or the summary thereof, and IAAC's draft Impact Assessment Report and potential conditions.
Share your thoughts on enhancing self-service Closed Communication | Industry | Regulations | Science and technology Nov 22, 2024 to Mar 12, 2025 2024-11-22 2025-03-12 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission We are seeking your views on how Canadians should be able to change their plans quickly and easily without having to speak to a customer service representative.
Proposed Amendments to the Antarctic Environmental Protection Regulations Closed Regulations Feb 8, 2025 to Mar 11, 2025 2025-02-08 2025-03-11 Environment and Climate Change Canada Obtain comments and feedback from the regulated community in particular, and Canadians in general, on the proposed amendments to the Antarctic Environmental Protection Regulations.
Sidewalk addition at NRC Uplands Campus Closed Environment | Science and technology Feb 5, 2025 to Mar 11, 2025 2025-02-05 2025-03-11 National Research Council Canada The National Research Council of Canada (NRC) is proposing the construction of a sidewalk addition to provide accessible pedestrian access from the intersection of Levy Private and Sonic Private, and the intersection of Research Private and Sonic Private, to various research facilities within the NRC's Uplands Campus (buildings U61, U62, U72, U66 and U70). The NRC must determine whether the proposed sidewalk addition, located at the NRC Uplands Campus, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, is likely to cause significant adverse environmental effects. To help inform this determination, the NRC is inviting comments from the public respecting that determination.
Pre-budget Consultations 2025 Closed Economy Feb 14, 2025 to Mar 10, 2025 2025-02-14 2025-03-10 Department of Finance Canada Pre-budget consultations are an opportunity for Canadians from across the country to contribute to shaping the Government of Canada's policies and priorities for Canada's future. What is most important to you? What additional actions should the government take in light of potential U.S. tariffs on Canadian goods? Join the conversation by taking our questionnaire below or sending in a formal submission, both of which are available until March 10, 2025.
Consultation on Wiinipaakw Indigenous Protected Area and National Marine Conservation Area Closed Animals | Culture | Development | Environment | Indigenous Peoples | Plants Feb 25, 2025 to Mar 9, 2025 2025-02-25 2025-03-09 Parks Canada Parks Canada and the Cree Nation Government are pleased to be reaching out to Canadians in order to understand their views related to the proposed Wiinipaakw Indigenous Protected Area (IPA) and National Marine Conservation Area (NMCA). This proposed protected area is located in the Eeyou Marine Region, in offshore areas of Eastern James Bay. This web survey is intended for both Indigenous and non-Indigenous individuals who are interested in commenting. This survey complements other engagement and consultation activities, including in-person meetings on this initiative.
Share your views: Consultation on proposed amendments to regulations to address health product shortages in Canada Closed Food and drug | Health Dec 28, 2024 to Mar 8, 2025 2024-12-28 2025-03-08 Health Canada Health Canada is proposing to amend the Food and Drug Regulations and Medical Devices Regulations to help prevent and mitigate the risk of harm to human health due to drug and medical device shortages and discontinuations. The proposed amendments: are outlined in the Regulations Amending Certain Regulations Made Under the Food and Drugs Act (Shortages and Discontinuation of Sale of Drugs and Medical Devices) were published for consultation in the Canada Gazette, Part I, on December 28, 2024
Department of Finance Consultation on Official Development Assistance, 2025 Closed International Jan 27, 2025 to Mar 7, 2025 2025-01-27 2025-03-07 Department of Finance Canada The consultation on Official Development Assistance disbursements provided stakeholders with an opportunity to comment on whether the Department’s current international assistance payments meet the criteria stated under the Official Development Assistance Accountability Act.
Consultation on proposed amendments to the Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR) Closed Transportation Dec 21, 2024 to Mar 6, 2025 2024-12-21 2025-03-06 Canadian Transportation Agency Proposed amendments to the Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR) have been published in the Canada Gazette, Part I, for consultation.
Notice to Stakeholders – Consultation on the Impacts of Implementation of ICH M13A: Bioequivalence for Immediate Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms Closed Health | Regulations Jan 17, 2025 to Mar 3, 2025 2025-01-17 2025-03-03 Health Canada Health Canada is seeking feedback on the harmonization of bioequivalence standards by application of the standards detailed in the ICH M13A guideline throughout the Comparative Bioavailability Standards: Formulations Used for Systemic Effects guidance document, and in particular, extending the application of the bioequivalence standards outlined in ICH M13A to modified-release products.
Consultation: Proposal to Amend the Regulations for Precursors and Designated Devices Closed Food and drug | Health Jan 31, 2025 to Mar 3, 2025 2025-01-31 2025-03-03 Health Canada On February 1, 2025, Health Canada published a Notice of Intent in the Canada Gazette, Part I, proposing to advance amendments to the Precursor Control Regulations and Schedule IX to the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act that would increase oversight of precursors, strengthen Canada's regulatory framework, and increase Health Canada's regulatory flexibility and agility.
Consultation on Proposed Amendments to the Export of Substances on the Export Control List Regulations Closed Amendments | Exporting/Importing | Health | International | Regulations Dec 16, 2024 to Mar 1, 2025 2024-12-16 2025-03-01 Environment and Climate Change Canada The purpose of this consultation is to solicit feedback from stakeholders and interested individuals regarding the proposals to amend the Export of Substances on the Export Control List Regulations.
Rose Lithium-Tantalum Mining Project Public Notice Public Comments Invited on Proposed Amendments to the Decision Statement Closed Economy | Economic development | Employment | Environment | Fisheries | Gender Based Analysis | Gender | Health | Indigenous Peoples | Natural resources | Society Jan 30, 2025 to Mar 1, 2025 2025-01-30 2025-03-01 Impact Assessment Agency of Canada January 30, 2025 — The Impact Assessment Agency of Canada (IAAC) is holding a public comment period as part of the post-decision phase for the Rose Lithium-Tantalum Mining Project, an open-pit lithium and tantalum mine located north of the Cree Nation of Nemaska, Quebec, in the Eeyou Istchee territory.
Share your thoughts about removing barriers to switching plans Closed Communication | Industry | Regulations | Science and technology Nov 22, 2024 to Feb 28, 2025 2024-11-22 2025-02-28 Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission We are looking for your views on banning fees that may prevent Canadians from changing or cancelling their cellphone or Internet plans.
